2: 3: 4: 5: 6: 7: 8: One delightful Ruby framework for every major AI provider. Build AI agents, chatbots, RAG apps, and multimodal workflows in beautiful, expressive code. 9: 10: 11: Battle tested at [](https://chatwithwork.com) - *Fully private work AI* 12: 13: [![Gem Version](https://badge.fury.io/rb/ruby_llm.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/rb/ruby_llm) 14: [![Ruby Style Guide](https://img.shields.io/badge/code_style-rubocop-brightgreen.svg)](https://github.com/rubocop/rubocop) 15: [![Gem Downloads](https://img.shields.io/gem/dt/ruby_llm)](https://rubygems.org/gems/ruby_llm) 16: [![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/crmne/ruby_llm/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/crmne/ruby_llm) 17: 18: 19:
20: 21: > [!NOTE] 22: > Using RubyLLM? [Share your story](https://tally.so/r/3Na02p)! Takes 5 minutes. 23: 24: --- 25: 26: Build chatbots, AI agents, RAG applications. Works with OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, Google, AWS, local models, and any OpenAI-compatible API. 27: 28: ## Build a working Ruby AI chat in two minutes 29: 30: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/65422091-9338-47da-a303-92b918bd1345 31: 32: ## Why RubyLLM? 33: 34: Every AI provider ships their own bloated client. Different APIs. Different response formats. Different conventions. It's exhausting. 35: 36: RubyLLM gives you one beautiful framework for all of them. Same interface whether you're using GPT, Claude, or your local Ollama. Just three dependencies: Faraday, Zeitwerk, and Marcel. That's it. 37: 38: ## Show me the code 39: 40: ```ruby 41: # Just ask questions 42: chat = RubyLLM.chat 43: chat.ask "What's the best way to learn Ruby?" 44: ``` 45: 46: ```ruby 47: # Analyze any file type 48: chat.ask "What's in this image?", with: "ruby_conf.jpg" 49: chat.ask "What's happening in this video?", with: "video.mp4" 50: chat.ask "Describe this meeting", with: "meeting.wav" 51: chat.ask "Summarize this document", with: "contract.pdf" 52: chat.ask "Explain this code", with: "app.rb" 53: ``` 54: 55: ```ruby 56: # Multiple files at once 57: chat.ask "Analyze these files", with: ["diagram.png", "report.pdf", "notes.txt"] 58: ``` 59: 60: ```ruby 61: # Stream responses 62: chat.ask "Tell me a story about Ruby" do |chunk| 63: print chunk.content 64: end 65: ``` 66: 67: ```ruby 68: # Generate images 69: RubyLLM.paint "a sunset over mountains in watercolor style" 70: ``` 71: 72: ```ruby 73: # Create embeddings 74: RubyLLM.embed "Ruby is elegant and expressive" 75: ``` 76: 77: ```ruby 78: # Transcribe audio to text 79: RubyLLM.transcribe "meeting.wav" 80: ``` 81: 82: ```ruby 83: # Moderate content for safety 84: RubyLLM.moderate "Check if this text is safe" 85: ``` 86: 87: ```ruby 88: # Let AI use your code 89: class Weather < RubyLLM::Tool 90: desc "Get current weather" 91: 92: def execute(latitude:, longitude:) 93: url = "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=#{latitude}&longitude=#{longitude}¤t=temperature_2m,wind_speed_10m" 94: JSON.parse(Faraday.get(url).body) 95: end 96: end 97: 98: chat.with_tool(Weather).ask "What's the weather in Berlin?" 99: ``` 100: (Showing lines 1-100 of 194. 1: # frozen_string_literal: true

6: module RubyLLM
7:   # Base class for LLM providers. A provider knows where to talk (host, auth,
8:   # configuration) and which protocol to speak for a given model and request.
9:   # The protocols themselves live under RubyLLM::Protocols.
10:   class Provider
11:     attr_reader :config, :connection
12: 
13:     def initialize(config)
14:       @config = config
15:       ensure_configured!
16:       @connection = Connection.new(self, @config)
17:     end
18: 
19:     def api_base
20:       raise NotImplementedError
21:     end
22: 
23:     def headers
24:       {}
25:     end
26: 
27:     def slug
28:       self.class.slug
29:     end
30: 
31:     def name
32:       self.class.name
33:     end
34: 
35:     def capabilities
36:       self.class.capabilities
37:     end
38: 
39:     def configuration_requirements
40:       self.class.configuration_requirements
41:     end
42: 
43:     def protocols
44:       self.class.protocols
45:     end
46: 
47:     # Routing hook. Override to pick a protocol per model or request —
48:     # an explicit `with_protocol` or `config.<slug>_protocol` wins over this.
49:     def protocol_for(_model, **)
50:       default_protocol
51:     end
52: 
53:     # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
54:     def complete(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, params: {}, headers: {}, schema: nil, thinking: nil,
55:                  citations: false, tool_prefs: nil, protocol: nil, &)
56:       protocol_class = resolve_protocol(protocol, model, tools:, schema:, thinking:, tool_prefs:, citations:)
57:       protocol_class.new(self, model).complete(
58:         messages,
59:         tools: tools,
60:         tool_prefs: tool_prefs,
61:         temperature: temperature,
62:         params: params,
63:         headers: headers,
64:         schema: schema,
65:         thinking: thinking,
66:         citations: citations,
67:         &
68:       )
69:     end
70:     # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
71: 
72:     def list_models
73:       default_protocol.new(self).list_models
74:     end
75: 
76:     def embed(text, model:, dimensions:)
77:       default_protocol.new(self).embed(text, model:, dimensions:)
78:     end
79: 
80:     def moderate(input, model:)
81:       default_protocol.new(self).moderate(input, model:)
82:     end
83: 
84:     def paint(prompt, model:, size:, with: nil, mask: nil, params: {}) # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
85:       default_protocol.new(self).paint(prompt, model:, size:, with:, mask:, params:)
86:     end
87: 
88:     def transcribe(audio_file, model:, language:, **options)
89:       default_protocol.new(self).transcribe(audio_file, model:, language:, **options)
90:     end
91: 
92:     def configured?
93:       self.class.configured?(@config)
94:     end
95: 
96:     def local?
97:       self.class.local?
98:     end
99: 
100:     def assume_models_exist?
101:       self.class.assume_models_exist?
102:     end
103: 
104:     def parse_error(response)
105:       return if response.body.empty?
106: 
107:       body = try_parse_json(response.body)
108:       case body
109:       when Hash
110:         error = body['error']
111:         return error if error.is_a?(String)
112: 
113:         [body.dig('error', 'message'), body['message'], body['detail']].find do |message|
114:           message.is_a?(String)
115:         end
116:       when Array
117:         body.map do |part|
118:           error = part['error']
119:           error.is_a?(String) ? error : part.dig('error', 'message')
120:         end.join('. ')
121:       else
122:         body
123:       end
124:     end
125: 
126:     class << self
127:       def name
128:         to_s.split('::').last
129:       end
130: 
131:       def slug
132:         name.downcase
133:       end
134: 
135:       def capabilities
136:         nil
137:       end
138: 
139:       def configuration_requirements
140:         []
141:       end
142: 
143:       def configuration_options
144:         []
145:       end
146: 
147:       def local?
148:         false
149:       end
150: 
151:       def remote?
152:         !local?
153:       end
154: 
155:       def assume_models_exist?
156:         false
157:       end
158: 
159:       def configured?(config)
160:         configuration_requirements.all? { |req| config.send(req) }
161:       end
162: 
163:       def protocol(name, protocol_class)
164:         @default_protocol = name.to_sym if protocols.empty?
165:         protocols[name.to_sym] = protocol_class
166:       end
167: 
168:       def protocols
169:         @protocols ||= {}
170:       end
171: 
172:       attr_reader :default_protocol
173: 
174:       def register(name, provider_class)
175:         providers[name.to_sym] = provider_class
176:         RubyLLM::Configuration.register_provider_options(provider_class.configuration_options + [:"#{name}_protocol"])
177:       end
178: 
179:       def resolve(name)
180:         providers[name.to_sym]
181:       end
182: 
183:       def providers
184:         @providers ||= {}
185:       end
186: 
187:       def local_providers
188:         providers.select { |_slug, provider_class| provider_class.local? }
189:       end
190: 
191:       def remote_providers
192:         providers.select { |_slug, provider_class| provider_class.remote? }
193:       end
194: 
195:       def configured_providers(config)
196:         providers.select do |_slug, provider_class|
197:           provider_class.configured?(config)
198:         end.values
199:       end
200: 
201:       def configured_remote_providers(config)
202:         providers.select do |_slug, provider_class|
203:           provider_class.remote? && provider_class.configured?(config)
204:         end.values
205:       end
206:     end
207: 
208:     private
209: 
210:     def resolve_protocol(name, model, **request)
211:       explicit = name || configured_protocol
212:       explicit ? fetch_protocol(explicit) : protocol_for(model, **request)
213:     end
214: 
215:     def default_protocol
216:       fetch_protocol(configured_protocol || self.class.default_protocol)
217:     end
218: 
219:     def configured_protocol
220:       @config.send(:"#{slug}_protocol")
221:     end
222: 
223:     def fetch_protocol(name)
224:       protocols.fetch(name.to_sym) do
225:         raise Error, "#{name} is not a protocol of #{self.class.name}. Available: #{protocols.keys.join(', ')}"
226:       end
227:     end
228: 
229:     def try_parse_json(maybe_json)
230:       return maybe_json unless maybe_json.is_a?(String)
231: 
232:       JSON.parse(maybe_json)
233:     rescue JSON::ParserError
234:       maybe_json
235:     end
236: 
237:     def ensure_configured!
238:       return if configured?
239: 
240:       missing = configuration_requirements.reject { |req| @config.send(req) }
241:       config_block = <<~RUBY
242:         RubyLLM.configure do |config|
243:           #{missing.map { |key| "config.#{key} = ENV['#{key.to_s.upcase}']" }.join("\n  ")}
244:         end
245:       RUBY
246: 
247:       raise ConfigurationError,
248:             "#{name} provider is not configured. Add this to your initialization:\n\n#{config_block}"
249:     end
250:   end
251: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'logger'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   # Global configuration for RubyLLM
7:   class Configuration
8:     class << self
9:       # Declare a single configuration option.
10:       def option(key, default = nil)
11:         key = key.to_sym
12:         return if options.include?(key)
13: 
14:         attr_reader key
15: 
16:         define_method("#{key}=") do |value|
17:           value = nil if value.is_a?(String) && value.strip.empty?
18:           instance_variable_set(:"@#{key}", value)
19:         end
20: 
21:         option_keys << key
22:         defaults[key] = default
23:       end
24: 
25:       def register_provider_options(options)
26:         Array(options).each { |key| option(key, nil) }
27:       end
28: 
29:       def options
30:         option_keys.dup
31:       end
32: 
33:       private
34: 
35:       def option_keys = @option_keys ||= []
36:       def defaults = @defaults ||= {}
37:       private :option
38:     end
39: 
40:     # System-level options are declared here.
41:     # Provider-specific options are declared in each provider class via
42:     # `self.configuration_options` and registered through Provider.register.
43:     option :default_model, 'gpt-5.4'
44:     option :default_embedding_model, 'text-embedding-3-small'
45:     option :default_moderation_model, 'omni-moderation-latest'
46:     option :default_image_model, 'gpt-image-1.5'
47:     option :default_transcription_model, 'whisper-1'
48: 
49:     option :model_registry_file, -> { File.expand_path('models.json', __dir__) }
50:     option :model_registry_class, 'Model'
51: 
52:     option :use_new_acts_as, true
53:     option :model_registry_source, nil
54: 
55:     option :request_timeout, 300
56:     option :max_retries, 3
57:     option :retry_interval, 0.1
58:     option :retry_backoff_factor, 2
59:     option :retry_interval_randomness, 0.5
60:     option :http_proxy, nil
61:     option :tool_concurrency, false
62: 
63:     option :logger, nil
64:     option :instrumenter, nil
65:     option :deprecation_behavior, :warn
66:     option :faraday_adapter, :net_http
67:     option :log_file, -> { $stdout }
68:     option :log_level, -> { ENV['RUBYLLM_DEBUG'] ? Logger::DEBUG : Logger::INFO }
69:     option :log_stream_debug, -> { ENV['RUBYLLM_STREAM_DEBUG'] == 'true' }
70:     option :log_regexp_timeout, -> { Regexp.respond_to?(:timeout) ? (Regexp.timeout || 1.0) : nil }
71: 
72:     def initialize
73:       self.class.send(:defaults).each do |key, default|
74:         value = default.respond_to?(:call) ? instance_exec(&default) : default
75:         public_send("#{key}=", value)
76:       end
77:     end
78: 
79:     def instance_variables
80:       super.reject { |ivar| ivar.to_s.match?(/(_id|_key|_secret|_token)$/) }
81:     end
82: 
83:     def log_regexp_timeout=(value)
84:       if value && !Regexp.respond_to?(:timeout)
85:         RubyLLM.logger.warn("log_regexp_timeout is not supported on Ruby #{RUBY_VERSION}")
86:       end
87:       @log_regexp_timeout = value
88:     end
89:   end
90: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   # Base class for wire protocols. A protocol knows how to talk to a family
5:   # of APIs: rendering payloads, parsing responses, streaming chunks, and the
6:   # endpoints involved. Providers know where to talk and who they are.
7:   class Protocol
8:     include Streaming
9: 
10:     attr_reader :provider, :config, :connection, :model
11: 
12:     def initialize(provider, model = nil)
13:       @provider = provider
14:       @config = provider.config
15:       @connection = provider.connection
16:       @model = model
17:     end
18: 
19:     # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
20:     def complete(messages, tools:, temperature:, params: {}, headers: {}, schema: nil, thinking: nil,
21:                  citations: false, tool_prefs: nil, &)
22:       normalized_temperature = maybe_normalize_temperature(temperature, model)
23: 
24:       payload = Utils.deep_merge(
25:         render_payload(
26:           messages,
27:           tools: tools,
28:           tool_prefs: tool_prefs,
29:           temperature: normalized_temperature,
30:           model: model,
31:           stream: block_given?,
32:           schema: schema,
33:           thinking: thinking,
34:           citations: citations
35:         ),
36:         params
37:       )
38: 
39:       if block_given?
40:         stream_response payload, headers, &
41:       else
42:         sync_response payload, headers
43:       end
44:     end
45:     # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
46: 
47:     def list_models
48:       response = @connection.get models_url
49:       parse_list_models_response response, @provider.slug, @provider.capabilities
50:     end
51: 
52:     def embed(text, model:, dimensions:)
53:       payload = render_embedding_payload(text, model:, dimensions:)
54:       response = @connection.post(embedding_url(model:), payload)
55:       parse_embedding_response(response, model:, text:)
56:     end
57: 
58:     def moderate(input, model:)
59:       payload = render_moderation_payload(input, model:)
60:       response = @connection.post moderation_url, payload
61:       parse_moderation_response(response, model:)
62:     end
63: 
64:     def paint(prompt, model:, size:, with: nil, mask: nil, params: {}) # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
65:       validate_paint_inputs!(with:, mask:)
66:       payload = render_image_payload(prompt, model:, size:, with:, mask:, params:)
67:       response = @connection.post images_url(with:, mask:), payload
68:       parse_image_response(response, model:)
69:     end
70: 
71:     def transcribe(audio_file, model:, language:, **options)
72:       file_part = build_audio_file_part(audio_file)
73:       payload = render_transcription_payload(file_part, model:, language:, **options)
74:       response = @connection.post transcription_url, payload
75:       parse_transcription_response(response, model:)
76:     end
77: 
78:     def maybe_normalize_temperature(temperature, _model)
79:       temperature
80:     end
81: 
82:     def parse_error(response)
83:       @provider.parse_error(response)
84:     end
85: 
86:     private
87: 
88:     def validate_paint_inputs!(with:, mask:)
89:       return if with.nil? && mask.nil?
90: 
91:       raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, 'image reference'
92:     end
93: 
94:     def build_audio_file_part(file_path)
95:       require 'faraday/multipart'
96:       require 'marcel'
97:       require 'pathname'
98: 
99:       expanded_path = File.expand_path(file_path)
100:       mime_type = Marcel::MimeType.for(Pathname.new(expanded_path))
101: 
102:       Faraday::Multipart::FilePart.new(
103:         expanded_path,
104:         mime_type,
105:         File.basename(expanded_path)
106:       )
107:     end
108: 
109:     def sync_response(payload, additional_headers = {})
110:       response = @connection.post completion_url, payload do |req|
111:         req.headers = additional_headers.merge(req.headers) unless additional_headers.empty?
112:       end
113:       parse_completion_response response
114:     end
115:   end
116: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'date'
4: require 'json'
5: 
6: module RubyLLM
7:   # Registry of available AI models and their capabilities.
8:   class Models
9:     include Enumerable
10: 
11:     MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP = {
12:       'openai' => 'openai',
13:       'anthropic' => 'anthropic',
14:       'google' => 'gemini',
15:       'google-vertex' => 'vertexai',
16:       'amazon-bedrock' => 'bedrock',
17:       'deepseek' => 'deepseek',
18:       'mistral' => 'mistral',
19:       'openrouter' => 'openrouter',
20:       'perplexity' => 'perplexity',
21:       'xai' => 'xai'
22:     }.freeze
23:     MODELS_DEV_INPUT_MODALITIES = %w[text image audio pdf video file].freeze
24:     MODELS_DEV_OUTPUT_MODALITIES = %w[text image audio video embeddings moderation].freeze
25:     MODELS_DEV_AUTHORITY_CAPABILITIES = %w[function_calling structured_output reasoning vision].freeze
26:     PROVIDER_PREFERENCE = %w[
27:       openai
28:       anthropic
29:       gemini
30:       vertexai
31:       bedrock
32:       openrouter
33:       deepseek
34:       mistral
35:       perplexity
36:       xai
37:       azure
38:       ollama
39:       gpustack
40:     ].freeze
41:     INSTANCE_DELEGATES = (Enumerable.instance_methods(false) + %i[
42:       all
43:       each
44:       find
45:       chat_models
46:       embedding_models
47:       audio_models
48:       image_models
49:       by_family
50:       by_provider
51:       load_from_json!
52:       load_from_database!
53:       save_to_json
54:     ]).uniq.freeze
55: 
56:     class << self
57:       INSTANCE_DELEGATES.each do |method_name|
58:         define_method(method_name) do |*args, **kwargs, &block|
59:           instance.public_send(method_name, *args, **kwargs, &block)
60:         end
61:       end
62: 
63:       def instance
64:         @instance ||= new
65:       end
66: 
67:       def schema_file
68:         File.expand_path('models_schema.json', __dir__)
69:       end
70: 
71:       def load_models(file = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file)
72:         source = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_source
73:         if source && file == RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file
74:           models = source.read
75:           return models if models.any?
76: 
77:           RubyLLM.logger.debug { 'Model registry source is empty, falling back to JSON registry' }
78:         end
79: 
80:         read_from_json(file)
81:       end
82: 
83:       def read_from_json(file = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file)
84:         data = File.exist?(file) ? File.read(file) : '[]'
85:         models = JSON.parse(data, symbolize_names: true).map { |model| Model::Info.new(model) }
86:         filter_models(models)
87:       rescue JSON::ParserError
88:         []
89:       end
90: 
91:       def read_from_database
92:         ModelRegistry::ActiveRecordSource.new.read
93:       end
94: 
95:       def refresh!(remote_only: false)
96:         instance.refresh!(remote_only: remote_only)
97:       end
98: 
99:       # Fetches and merges the latest models. Call save_to_json when the
100:       # refreshed registry should also be persisted.
101:       def fetch_merged_models(remote_only: false)
102:         RubyLLM.instrument('models.refresh.ruby_llm', remote_only:) do |payload|
103:           existing_models = read_existing_models
104: 
105:           provider_fetch = fetch_provider_models(remote_only: remote_only)
106:           log_provider_fetch(provider_fetch)
107: 
108:           models_dev_fetch = fetch_models_dev_models(existing_models)
109:           log_models_dev_fetch(models_dev_fetch)
110: 
111:           merged_models = merge_with_existing(existing_models, provider_fetch, models_dev_fetch)
112:           payload[:model_count] = merged_models.size
113:           merged_models
114:         end
115:       end
116: 
117:       def fetch_provider_models(remote_only: true)
118:         config = RubyLLM.config
119:         providers = remote_only ? Provider.configured_remote_providers(config) : Provider.configured_providers(config)
120:         result = { models: [], fetched_providers: [], configured_names: providers.map(&:name), failed: [] }
121: 
122:         providers.each do |provider_class|
123:           result[:models].concat(provider_class.new(config).list_models)
124:           result[:fetched_providers] << provider_class.slug
125:         rescue StandardError => e
126:           result[:failed] << { name: provider_class.name, slug: provider_class.slug, error: e }
127:         end
128: 
129:         result
130:       end
131: 
132:       def resolve(model_id, provider: nil, assume_exists: false, config: nil) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
133:         config ||= RubyLLM.config
134:         provider_class = provider ? Provider.providers[provider.to_sym] : nil
135:         assume_exists = true if provider_class&.local? || provider_class&.assume_models_exist?
136: 
137:         if assume_exists
138:           raise ArgumentError, 'Provider must be specified if assume_exists is true' unless provider
139: 
140:           provider_class ||= raise_unknown_provider(provider)
141: 
142:           model = if provider_class.local?
143:                     begin
144:                       Models.find(model_id, provider)
145:                     rescue ModelNotFoundError
146:                       nil
147:                     end
148:                   end
149: 
150:           model ||= Model::Info.default(model_id, provider_class.slug)
151:         else
152:           model = Models.find model_id, provider
153:           provider_class = Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym] || raise_unknown_provider(model.provider)
154:         end
155:         [model, provider_class.new(config)]
156:       end
157: 
158:       def fetch_models_dev_models(existing_models) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
159:         RubyLLM.logger.info 'Fetching models from models.dev API...'
160: 
161:         connection = Connection.basic do |f|
162:           f.request :json
163:           f.response :json, parser_options: { symbolize_names: true }
164:         end
165:         response = connection.get 'https://models.dev/api.json'
166:         providers = response.body || {}
167: 
168:         models = providers.flat_map do |provider_key, provider_data|
169:           provider_slug = MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP[provider_key.to_s]
170:           next [] unless provider_slug
171: 
172:           (provider_data[:models] || {}).values.map do |model_data|
173:             Model::Info.new(models_dev_model_to_info(model_data, provider_slug, provider_key.to_s))
174:           end
175:         end
176:         { models: models.reject { |model| model.provider.nil? || model.id.nil? }, fetched: true }
177:       rescue StandardError => e
178:         RubyLLM.logger.warn("Failed to fetch models.dev (#{e.class}: #{e.message}). Keeping existing.")
179:         {
180:           models: existing_models.select { |model| model.metadata[:source] == 'models.dev' },
181:           fetched: false
182:         }
183:       end
184: 
185:       def read_existing_models
186:         existing_models = instance&.all
187:         existing_models = read_from_json if existing_models.nil? || existing_models.empty?
188:         existing_models
189:       end
190: 
191:       def raise_unknown_provider(provider)
192:         available = Provider.providers.keys.join(', ')
193:         raise Error, "Unknown provider: #{provider.inspect}. Available providers: #{available}"
194:       end
195: 
196:       def log_provider_fetch(provider_fetch)
197:         RubyLLM.logger.info "Fetching models from providers: #{provider_fetch[:configured_names].join(', ')}"
198:         provider_fetch[:failed].each do |failure|
199:           RubyLLM.logger.warn(
200:             "Failed to fetch #{failure[:name]} models (#{failure[:error].class}: #{failure[:error].message}). " \
201:             'Keeping existing.'
202:           )
203:         end
204:       end
205: 
206:       def log_models_dev_fetch(models_dev_fetch)
207:         return if models_dev_fetch[:fetched]
208: 
209:         RubyLLM.logger.warn('Using cached models.dev data due to fetch failure.')
210:       end
211: 
212:       def merge_with_existing(existing_models, provider_fetch, models_dev_fetch)
213:         existing_by_provider = existing_models.group_by(&:provider)
214:         preserved_models = existing_by_provider
215:                            .except(*provider_fetch[:fetched_providers])
216:                            .values
217:                            .flatten
218: 
219:         provider_models = provider_fetch[:models] + preserved_models
220:         models_dev_models = if models_dev_fetch[:fetched]
221:                               models_dev_fetch[:models]
222:                             else
223:                               existing_models.select { |model| model.metadata[:source] == 'models.dev' }
224:                             end
225: 
226:         merge_models(provider_models, models_dev_models)
227:       end
228: 
229:       def merge_models(provider_models, models_dev_models)
230:         models_dev_by_key = index_by_key(models_dev_models)
231:         provider_by_key = index_by_key(provider_models)
232: 
233:         all_keys = models_dev_by_key.keys | provider_by_key.keys
234: 
235:         models = all_keys.map do |key|
236:           models_dev_model = find_models_dev_model(key, models_dev_by_key)
237:           provider_model = provider_by_key[key]
238: 
239:           if models_dev_model && provider_model
240:             add_provider_metadata(models_dev_model, provider_model)
241:           elsif models_dev_model
242:             models_dev_model
243:           else
244:             provider_model
245:           end
246:         end
247: 
248:         filter_models(models).sort_by { |m| [m.provider, m.id] }
249:       end
250: 
251:       def filter_models(models)
252:         models.reject do |model|
253:           model.provider.to_s == 'vertexai' && model.id.to_s.include?('/')
254:         end
255:       end
256: 
257:       def find_models_dev_model(key, models_dev_by_key)
258:         # Direct match
259:         return models_dev_by_key[key] if models_dev_by_key[key]
260: 
261:         provider, model_id = key.split(':', 2)
262:         if provider == 'bedrock'
263:           normalized_id = model_id.sub(/^[a-z]{2}\./, '')
264:           context_override = nil
265:           normalized_id = normalized_id.gsub(/:(\d+)k\b/) do
266:             context_override = Regexp.last_match(1).to_i * 1000
267:             ''
268:           end
269:           bedrock_model = models_dev_by_key["bedrock:#{normalized_id}"]
270:           if bedrock_model
271:             data = bedrock_model.to_h.merge(id: model_id)
272:             data[:context_window] = context_override if context_override
273:             return Model::Info.new(data)
274:           end
275:         end
276: 
277:         # VertexAI uses same models as Gemini
278:         return unless provider == 'vertexai'
279: 
280:         gemini_model = models_dev_by_key["gemini:#{model_id}"]
281:         return unless gemini_model
282: 
283:         # Return Gemini's models.dev data but with VertexAI as provider
284:         Model::Info.new(gemini_model.to_h.merge(provider: 'vertexai'))
285:       end
286: 
287:       def index_by_key(models)
288:         models.to_h do |model|
289:           ["#{model.provider}:#{model.id}", model]
290:         end
291:       end
292: 
293:       def add_provider_metadata(models_dev_model, provider_model) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
294:         data = models_dev_model.to_h
295:         data[:name] = provider_model.name if blank_value?(data[:name])
296:         data[:family] = provider_model.family if blank_value?(data[:family])
297:         data[:created_at] = provider_model.created_at if blank_value?(data[:created_at])
298:         data[:context_window] = provider_model.context_window if blank_value?(data[:context_window])
299:         data[:max_output_tokens] = provider_model.max_output_tokens if blank_value?(data[:max_output_tokens])
300:         data[:modalities] = provider_model.modalities.to_h if blank_value?(data[:modalities])
301:         data[:pricing] = provider_model.pricing.to_h if blank_value?(data[:pricing])
302:         data[:metadata] = provider_model.metadata.merge(data[:metadata] || {})
303:         provider_capabilities = provider_model.capabilities - MODELS_DEV_AUTHORITY_CAPABILITIES
304:         data[:capabilities] = (models_dev_model.capabilities + provider_capabilities).uniq
305:         normalize_embedding_modalities(data)
306:         Model::Info.new(data)
307:       end
308: 
309:       def normalize_embedding_modalities(data)
310:         return unless data[:id].to_s.include?('embedding')
311: 
312:         modalities = data[:modalities].to_h
313:         modalities[:input] = ['text'] if modalities[:input].nil? || modalities[:input].empty?
314:         modalities[:output] = ['embeddings']
315:         data[:modalities] = modalities
316:       end
317: 
318:       def blank_value?(value)
319:         return true if value.nil?
320:         return value.empty? if value.is_a?(String) || value.is_a?(Array)
321: 
322:         if value.is_a?(Hash)
323:           return true if value.empty?
324: 
325:           return value.values.all? { |nested| blank_value?(nested) }
326:         end
327: 
328:         false
329:       end
330: 
331:       def models_dev_model_to_info(model_data, provider_slug, provider_key)
332:         modalities = normalize_models_dev_modalities(model_data[:modalities])
333:         capabilities = models_dev_capabilities(model_data, modalities)
334: 
335:         created_date = [model_data[:release_date], model_data[:last_updated]]
336:                        .find { |value| !value.to_s.strip.empty? }
337: 
338:         data = {
339:           id: model_data[:id],
340:           name: model_data[:name] || model_data[:id],
341:           provider: provider_slug,
342:           family: model_data[:family],
343:           created_at: Utils.iso_date_prefix_to_utc_midnight_string(created_date),
344:           context_window: model_data.dig(:limit, :context),
345:           max_output_tokens: model_data.dig(:limit, :output),
346:           knowledge_cutoff: normalize_models_dev_knowledge(model_data[:knowledge]),
347:           modalities: modalities,
348:           capabilities: capabilities,
349:           pricing: models_dev_pricing(model_data[:cost]),
350:           metadata: models_dev_metadata(model_data, provider_key)
351:         }
352: 
353:         normalize_embedding_modalities(data)
354:         data
355:       end
356: 
357:       def models_dev_capabilities(model_data, modalities)
358:         capabilities = []
359:         capabilities << 'function_calling' if model_data[:tool_call]
360:         capabilities << 'structured_output' if model_data[:structured_output]
361:         capabilities << 'reasoning' if model_data[:reasoning] || model_data[:reasoning_options]
362:         capabilities << 'vision' if modalities[:input].intersect?(%w[image video pdf])
363:         capabilities.uniq
364:       end
365: 
366:       def models_dev_pricing(cost)
367:         return {} unless cost
368: 
369:         text_standard = {
370:           input_per_million: cost[:input],
371:           output_per_million: cost[:output],
372:           cache_read_input_per_million: cost[:cache_read],
373:           cache_write_input_per_million: cost[:cache_write],
374:           reasoning_output_per_million: cost[:reasoning]
375:         }.compact
376: 
377:         audio_standard = {
378:           input_per_million: cost[:input_audio],
379:           output_per_million: cost[:output_audio]
380:         }.compact
381: 
382:         pricing = {}
383:         pricing[:text_tokens] = { standard: text_standard } if text_standard.any?
384:         pricing[:audio_tokens] = { standard: audio_standard } if audio_standard.any?
385:         pricing
386:       end
387: 
388:       def models_dev_metadata(model_data, provider_key)
389:         metadata = {
390:           source: 'models.dev',
391:           provider_id: provider_key,
392:           open_weights: model_data[:open_weights],
393:           attachment: model_data[:attachment],
394:           temperature: model_data[:temperature],
395:           last_updated: model_data[:last_updated],
396:           status: model_data[:status],
397:           interleaved: model_data[:interleaved],
398:           reasoning_options: model_data[:reasoning_options],
399:           cost: model_data[:cost],
400:           limit: model_data[:limit],
401:           knowledge: model_data[:knowledge]
402:         }
403:         metadata.compact
404:       end
405: 
406:       def normalize_models_dev_modalities(modalities)
407:         normalized = { input: [], output: [] }
408:         return normalized unless modalities
409: 
410:         normalized[:input] = Array(modalities[:input]).compact & MODELS_DEV_INPUT_MODALITIES
411:         normalized[:output] = Array(modalities[:output]).compact & MODELS_DEV_OUTPUT_MODALITIES
412:         normalized
413:       end
414: 
415:       def normalize_models_dev_knowledge(value)
416:         return if value.nil?
417:         return value if value.is_a?(Date)
418: 
419:         Date.parse(value.to_s)
420:       rescue ArgumentError
421:         nil
422:       end
423:     end
424: 
425:     def initialize(models = nil)
426:       @models = self.class.filter_models(models || self.class.load_models)
427:     end
428: 
429:     # Replaces this registry instance with models loaded from JSON.
430:     def load_from_json!(file = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file)
431:       @models = self.class.read_from_json(file)
432:     end
433: 
434:     # Replaces this registry instance with models loaded from the configured
435:     # ActiveRecord model class.
436:     def load_from_database!
437:       @models = self.class.read_from_database
438:     end
439: 
440:     # Persists this registry instance to JSON without changing the global
441:     # RubyLLM.models instance.
442:     def save_to_json(file = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_file)
443:       File.write(file, JSON.pretty_generate(all.map(&:to_h)))
444:     end
445: 
446:     def all
447:       @models
448:     end
449: 
450:     def each(&)
451:       all.each(&)
452:     end
453: 
454:     def find(model_id, provider = nil)
455:       if provider
456:         find_with_provider(model_id, provider)
457:       else
458:         find_without_provider(model_id)
459:       end
460:     end
461: 
462:     def chat_models
463:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.type == 'chat' })
464:     end
465: 
466:     def embedding_models
467:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.type == 'embedding' || m.modalities.output.include?('embeddings') })
468:     end
469: 
470:     def audio_models
471:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.type == 'audio' || m.modalities.output.include?('audio') })
472:     end
473: 
474:     def image_models
475:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.type == 'image' || m.modalities.output.include?('image') })
476:     end
477: 
478:     def by_family(family)
479:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.family == family.to_s })
480:     end
481: 
482:     def by_provider(provider)
483:       self.class.new(all.select { |m| m.provider == provider.to_s })
484:     end
485: 
486:     def refresh!(remote_only: false)
487:       @models = self.class.fetch_merged_models(remote_only: remote_only)
488:       self
489:     end
490: 
491:     def resolve(model_id, provider: nil, assume_exists: false, config: nil)
492:       self.class.resolve(model_id, provider: provider, assume_exists: assume_exists, config: config)
493:     end
494: 
495:     private
496: 
497:     def find_with_provider(model_id, provider)
498:       resolved_id = Aliases.resolve(model_id, provider)
499:       resolved_id = resolve_bedrock_region_id(resolved_id) if provider.to_s == 'bedrock'
500:       all.find { |m| m.id == resolved_id && m.provider == provider.to_s } ||
501:         all.find { |m| m.id == model_id && m.provider == provider.to_s } ||
502:         raise_model_not_found(model_id, provider: provider)
503:     end
504: 
505:     def resolve_bedrock_region_id(model_id)
506:       region = RubyLLM.config.bedrock_region.to_s
507:       return model_id if region.empty?
508: 
509:       candidate_id = Providers::Bedrock::Models.with_region_prefix(model_id, region)
510:       return model_id if candidate_id == model_id
511: 
512:       candidate = all.find { |m| m.provider == 'bedrock' && m.id == candidate_id }
513:       return model_id unless candidate
514: 
515:       inference_types = Array(candidate.metadata[:inference_types] || candidate.metadata['inference_types'])
516:       Providers::Bedrock::Models.normalize_inference_profile_id(model_id, inference_types, region)
517:     end
518: 
519:     def find_without_provider(model_id)
520:       exact_matches = all.select { |m| m.id == model_id }
521:       return preferred_match(exact_matches) if exact_matches.any?
522: 
523:       resolved_id = Aliases.resolve(model_id)
524:       alias_matches = all.select { |m| m.id == resolved_id }
525:       return preferred_match(alias_matches) if alias_matches.any?
526: 
527:       raise_model_not_found(model_id)
528:     end
529: 
530:     def raise_model_not_found(model_id, provider: nil)
531:       message = "Unknown model: #{model_id.inspect}"
532:       message = "#{message} for provider: #{provider.inspect}" if provider
533: 
534:       raise ModelNotFoundError, "#{message}. #{refresh_registry_guidance}"
535:     end
536: 
537:     def refresh_registry_guidance
538:       rails_model = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_class
539:       'If the model exists at the provider, refresh the registry with `RubyLLM.models.refresh!` ' \
540:         'and persist it with `RubyLLM.models.save_to_json`. ' \
541:         "Rails model registries can call `#{rails_model}.refresh!` instead."
542:     end
543: 
544:     def preferred_match(candidates)
545:       return candidates.first if candidates.size == 1
546: 
547:       candidates.min_by do |model|
548:         index = PROVIDER_PREFERENCE.index(model.provider)
549:         index || PROVIDER_PREFERENCE.length
550:       end
551:     end
552:   end
553: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   # Custom error class that wraps API errors from different providers
5:   # into a consistent format with helpful error messages.
6:   class Error < StandardError
7:     attr_reader :response
8: 
9:     def self.default_message
10:       nil
11:     end
12: 
13:     def initialize(response = nil, message = nil)
14:       if response.is_a?(String)
15:         message = response
16:         response = nil
17:       end
18: 
19:       @response = response
20:       super(message || response&.body || self.class.default_message)
21:     end
22:   end
23: 
24:   # Error classes for non-HTTP errors
25:   class ConfigurationError < StandardError; end
26:   class PromptNotFoundError < StandardError; end
27:   class InvalidRoleError < StandardError; end
28:   class InvalidToolChoiceError < StandardError; end
29:   class ModelNotFoundError < StandardError; end
30: 
31:   # Raised when RubyLLM cannot format an attachment for the selected provider.
32:   class UnsupportedAttachmentError < StandardError
33:     GUIDANCE = 'Consider using a model that supports this attachment type.'
34: 
35:     def initialize(type = nil)
36:       message = 'Unsupported attachment type'
37:       message = "#{message}: #{type}" if type
38:       super("#{message}. #{GUIDANCE}")
39:     end
40:   end
41: 
42:   # Error classes for different HTTP status codes
43:   # Raised when the API request is invalid.
44:   class BadRequestError < Error
45:     def self.default_message
46:       'Invalid request - please check your input'
47:     end
48:   end
49: 
50:   # Raised when the API key or account lacks permission for the request.
51:   class ForbiddenError < Error
52:     def self.default_message
53:       'Forbidden - you do not have permission to access this resource'
54:     end
55:   end
56: 
57:   # Raised when token or context limits are exceeded for a request.
58:   class ContextLengthExceededError < Error
59:     def self.default_message
60:       'Context length exceeded'
61:     end
62:   end
63: 
64:   # Raised when the service signals temporary overload.
65:   class OverloadedError < Error
66:     def self.default_message
67:       'Service overloaded - please try again later'
68:     end
69:   end
70: 
71:   # Raised when account billing prevents the request from being completed.
72:   class PaymentRequiredError < Error
73:     def self.default_message
74:       'Payment required - please top up your account'
75:     end
76:   end
77: 
78:   # Raised when rate limits are exceeded.
79:   class RateLimitError < Error
80:     def self.default_message
81:       'Rate limit exceeded - please wait a moment'
82:     end
83:   end
84: 
85:   # Raised when the provider returns a server-side error.
86:   class ServerError < Error
87:     def self.default_message
88:       'API server error - please try again'
89:     end
90:   end
91: 
92:   # Raised when the provider is temporarily unavailable.
93:   class ServiceUnavailableError < Error
94:     def self.default_message
95:       'API server unavailable - please try again later'
96:     end
97:   end
98: 
99:   # Raised when authentication fails.
100:   class UnauthorizedError < Error
101:     def self.default_message
102:       'Invalid API key - check your credentials'
103:     end
104:   end
105: end

3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     # OpenAI API integration.
6:     class OpenAI < Provider
7:       protocol :responses, Protocols::Responses
8:       protocol :chat_completions, Protocols::ChatCompletions
9: 
10:       def api_base
11:         @config.openai_api_base || 'https://api.openai.com/v1'
12:       end
13: 
14:       # Audio, realtime, and search-preview models only exist on Chat Completions.
15:       def protocol_for(model, **)
16:         model.id.match?(/audio|realtime|search-preview/) ? protocols[:chat_completions] : super
17:       end
18: 
19:       def headers
20:         {
21:           'Authorization' => "Bearer #{@config.openai_api_key}",
22:           'OpenAI-Organization' => @config.openai_organization_id,
23:           'OpenAI-Project' => @config.openai_project_id
24:         }.compact
25:       end
26: 
27:       class << self
28:         def capabilities
29:           OpenAI::Capabilities
30:         end
31: 
32:         def configuration_options
33:           %i[
34:             openai_api_key
35:             openai_api_base
36:             openai_organization_id
37:             openai_project_id
38:             openai_use_system_role
39:           ]
40:         end
41: 
42:         def configuration_requirements
43:           %i[openai_api_key]
44:         end
45:       end
46:     end
47:   end
48: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     # Native Gemini API implementation
6:     class Gemini < Provider
7:       protocol :gemini, Protocols::Gemini
8: 
9:       def api_base
10:         @config.gemini_api_base || 'https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta'
11:       end
12: 
13:       def headers
14:         {
15:           'x-goog-api-key' => @config.gemini_api_key
16:         }
17:       end
18: 
19:       class << self
20:         def capabilities
21:           Gemini::Capabilities
22:         end
23: 
24:         def configuration_options
25:           %i[gemini_api_key gemini_api_base]
26:         end
27: 
28:         def configuration_requirements
29:           %i[gemini_api_key]
30:         end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     # Azure AI Foundry / OpenAI-compatible API integration.
6:     class Azure < Provider
7:       protocol :chat_completions, Azure::ChatCompletions
8: 
9:       def api_base
10:         @config.azure_api_base
11:       end
12: 
13:       def headers
14:         if @config.azure_api_key
15:           { 'api-key' => @config.azure_api_key }
16:         else
17:           { 'Authorization' => "Bearer #{@config.azure_ai_auth_token}" }
18:         end
19:       end
20: 
21:       class << self
22:         def configuration_options
23:           %i[azure_api_base azure_api_key azure_ai_auth_token]
24:         end
25: 
26:         def configuration_requirements
27:           %i[azure_api_base]
28:         end
29: 
30:         def configured?(config)
31:           config.azure_api_base && (config.azure_api_key || config.azure_ai_auth_token)
32:         end
33: 
34:         # Azure works with deployment names, instead of model names
35:         def assume_models_exist?
36:           true
37:         end
38:       end
39: 
40:       def ensure_configured!
41:         missing = []
42:         missing << :azure_api_base unless @config.azure_api_base
43:         if @config.azure_api_key.nil? && @config.azure_ai_auth_token.nil?
44:           missing << 'azure_api_key or azure_ai_auth_token'
45:         end
46:         return if missing.empty?
47: 
48:         raise ConfigurationError,
49:               "Missing configuration for Azure: #{missing.join(', ')}"
50:       end
51:     end
52:   end
53: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     # AWS Bedrock integration.
6:     class Bedrock < Provider
7:       include Bedrock::Auth
8:       include Bedrock::Models
9: 
10:       protocol :converse, Protocols::Converse
11: 
12:       def api_base
13:         @config.bedrock_api_base || "https://bedrock-runtime.#{bedrock_region}.amazonaws.com"
14:       end
15: 
16:       def headers
17:         {}
18:       end
19: 
20:       def complete(messages, model:, params: {}, **rest, &)
21:         super(messages, model:, params: normalize_params(params, model:), **rest, &)
22:       end
23: 
24:       def parse_error(response)
25:         return if response.body.nil? || response.body.empty?
26: 
27:         body = try_parse_json(response.body)
28:         return body if body.is_a?(String)
29: 
30:         body['message'] || body['Message'] || body['error'] || body['__type'] || super
31:       end
32: 
33:       def list_models
34:         response = signed_get(models_api_base, models_url)
35:         parse_list_models_response(response, slug, capabilities)
36:       end
37: 
38:       class << self
39:         def configuration_options
40:           %i[bedrock_api_key bedrock_secret_key bedrock_region bedrock_session_token bedrock_api_base]
41:         end
42: 
43:         def configuration_requirements
44:           %i[bedrock_api_key bedrock_secret_key bedrock_region]
45:         end
46:       end
47: 
48:       private
49: 
50:       def bedrock_region
51:         @config.bedrock_region
52:       end
53: 
54:       def normalize_params(params, model:)
55:         normalized = RubyLLM::Utils.deep_symbolize_keys(params || {})
56:         additional_fields = normalized[:additionalModelRequestFields] || {}
57: 
58:         top_k = normalized.delete(:top_k)
59:         if !top_k.nil? && model_supports_top_k?(model)
60:           additional_fields = RubyLLM::Utils.deep_merge(additional_fields, { top_k: top_k })
61:         end
62: 
63:         normalized[:additionalModelRequestFields] = additional_fields unless additional_fields.empty?
64:         normalized
65:       end
66: 
67:       def model_supports_top_k?(model)
68:         Bedrock::Models.reasoning_embedded?(model)
69:       end
70:     end
71:   end
72: end

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'json'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   # Represents a conversation with an AI model
7:   class Chat
8:     include Enumerable
9: 
10:     attr_reader :model, :messages, :tools, :tool_prefs, :params, :headers, :schema, :concurrency
11: 
12:     def initialize(model: nil, provider: nil, assume_model_exists: false, context: nil)
13:       if assume_model_exists && !provider
14:         raise ArgumentError, 'Provider must be specified if assume_model_exists is true'
15:       end
16: 
17:       @context = context
18:       @config = context&.config || RubyLLM.config
19:       model_id = model || @config.default_model
20:       with_model(model_id, provider: provider, assume_exists: assume_model_exists)
21:       @temperature = nil
22:       @messages = []
23:       @tools = {}
24:       @tool_prefs = { choice: nil, calls: nil }
25:       @concurrency = normalize_tool_concurrency(@config.tool_concurrency)
26:       @params = {}
27:       @headers = {}
28:       @schema = nil
29:       @thinking = nil
30:       @citations = false
31:       @protocol = nil
32:       @on = {
33:         new_message: nil,
34:         end_message: nil,
35:         tool_call: nil,
36:         tool_result: nil
37:       }
38:       @callbacks = Hash.new { |callbacks, name| callbacks[name] = [] }
39:     end
40: 
41:     def ask(message = nil, with: nil, &)
42:       add_message role: :user, content: build_content(message, with)
43:       complete(&)
44:     end
45: 
46:     alias say ask
47: 
48:     def with_instructions(instructions, append: false, replace: nil)
49:       unless replace.nil?
50:         RubyLLM.deprecator.warn(
51:           '`replace:` is deprecated and will be removed in RubyLLM 2.0. ' \
52:           '`with_instructions` replaces by default; use `append: true` to append.'
53:         )
54:         append ||= replace == false
55:       end
56: 
57:       if append
58:         append_system_instruction(instructions)
59:       else
60:         replace_system_instruction(instructions)
61:       end
62: 
63:       self
64:     end
65: 
66:     def with_tool(tool, choice: nil, calls: nil, concurrency: @concurrency)
67:       unless tool.nil?
68:         tool_instance = tool.is_a?(Class) ? tool.new : tool
69:         @tools[tool_instance.name.to_sym] = tool_instance
70:       end
71:       update_tool_options(choice:, calls:)
72:       update_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
73:       self
74:     end
75: 
76:     def with_tools(*tools, replace: false, choice: nil, calls: nil, concurrency: @concurrency)
77:       @tools.clear if replace
78:       tools.compact.each { |tool| with_tool tool }
79:       update_tool_options(choice:, calls:)
80:       update_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
81:       self
82:     end
83: 
84:     def with_model(model_id, provider: nil, assume_exists: false)
85:       @model, @provider = Models.resolve(model_id, provider:, assume_exists:, config: @config)
86:       @connection = @provider.connection
87:       self
88:     end
89: 
90:     def with_temperature(temperature)
91:       @temperature = temperature
92:       self
93:     end
94: 
95:     def with_thinking(effort: nil, budget: nil)
96:       raise ArgumentError, 'with_thinking requires :effort or :budget' if effort.nil? && budget.nil?
97: 
98:       @thinking = Thinking::Config.new(effort: effort, budget: budget)
99:       self
100:     end
101: 
102:     def with_citations(enabled = true) # rubocop:disable Style/OptionalBooleanParameter
103:       @citations = enabled
104:       self
105:     end
106: 
107:     def with_context(context)
108:       @context = context
109:       @config = context.config
110:       with_model(@model.id, provider: @provider.slug, assume_exists: true)
111:       self
112:     end
113: 
114:     def with_params(**params)
115:       @params = params
116:       self
117:     end
118: 
119:     def with_protocol(protocol)
120:       @protocol = protocol
121:       self
122:     end
123: 
124:     def with_headers(**headers)
125:       @headers = headers
126:       self
127:     end
128: 
129:     def with_schema(schema)
130:       schema_instance = schema.is_a?(Class) ? schema.new : schema
131: 
132:       @schema = normalize_schema_payload(
133:         schema_instance.respond_to?(:to_json_schema) ? schema_instance.to_json_schema : schema_instance
134:       )
135: 
136:       self
137:     end
138: 
139:     def on_new_message(&)
140:       set_legacy_callback(:new_message, :on_new_message, :before_message, &)
141:     end
142: 
143:     def on_end_message(&)
144:       set_legacy_callback(:end_message, :on_end_message, :after_message, &)
145:     end
146: 
147:     def on_tool_call(&)
148:       set_legacy_callback(:tool_call, :on_tool_call, :before_tool_call, &)
149:     end
150: 
151:     def on_tool_result(&)
152:       set_legacy_callback(:tool_result, :on_tool_result, :after_tool_result, &)
153:     end
154: 
155:     def before_message(&)
156:       add_callback(:before_message, &)
157:     end
158: 
159:     def after_message(&)
160:       add_callback(:after_message, &)
161:     end
162: 
163:     def before_tool_call(&)
164:       add_callback(:before_tool_call, &)
165:     end
166: 
167:     def after_tool_result(&)
168:       add_callback(:after_tool_result, &)
169:     end
170: 
171:     def each(&)
172:       messages.each(&)
173:     end
174: 
175:     def cost
176:       Cost.aggregate(messages.map { |message| message.cost(model: message.model_info || model) })
177:     end
178: 
179:     def complete(&block)
180:       result = nil
181:       payload = {
182:         chat: self,
183:         provider: @provider.slug,
184:         provider_class: @provider.class.name,
185:         model: @model.id,
186:         model_info: @model,
187:         input_messages: messages.dup,
188:         message_count: messages.size,
189:         tools: tools.keys,
190:         tool_choice: tool_prefs[:choice],
191:         tool_call_limit: tool_prefs[:calls],
192:         temperature: @temperature,
193:         params: params,
194:         schema: schema,
195:         thinking: @thinking,
196:         citations: @citations,
197:         streaming: block_given?
198:       }
199: 
200:       RubyLLM.instrument('chat.ruby_llm', payload, config: @config) do |event|
201:         result = complete_once(&block)
202:         event[:response] = result
203:         event[:messages_after] = messages.dup
204:         event[:response_role] = result.role if result.respond_to?(:role)
205: 
206:         if result.respond_to?(:tool_call?)
207:           event[:response_model] = result.model_id
208:           event[:tool_call] = result.tool_call?
209:           event[:tool_calls] = result.tool_calls
210:           event[:input_tokens] = result.input_tokens
211:           event[:output_tokens] = result.output_tokens
212:           event[:cached_tokens] = result.cached_tokens
213:           event[:cache_creation_tokens] = result.cache_creation_tokens
214:           event[:thinking_tokens] = result.thinking_tokens
215:         end
216:       end
217:       result
218:     end
219: 
220:     def add_message(message_or_attributes)
221:       message = message_or_attributes.is_a?(Message) ? message_or_attributes : Message.new(message_or_attributes)
222:       messages << message
223:       message
224:     end
225: 
226:     # Mutates this chat by removing all in-memory messages.
227:     def reset_messages!
228:       @messages.clear
229:     end
230: 
231:     # Keeps the connection and config dumps out of pretty-printed output.
232:     def pretty_print_instance_variables
233:       super - %i[@connection @config]
234:     end
235: 
236:     private
237: 
238:     def normalize_schema_payload(raw_schema)
239:       return nil if raw_schema.nil?
240:       return raw_schema unless raw_schema.is_a?(Hash)
241: 
242:       schema = RubyLLM::Utils.deep_symbolize_keys(raw_schema)
243:       schema_def = extract_schema_definition(schema)
244:       strict = extract_schema_strict(schema, schema_def)
245:       build_schema_payload(schema, schema_def, strict)
246:     end
247: 
248:     def extract_schema_definition(schema)
249:       RubyLLM::Utils.deep_dup(schema[:schema] || schema)
250:     end
251: 
252:     def extract_schema_strict(schema, schema_def)
253:       return schema[:strict] if schema.key?(:strict)
254:       return schema_def.delete(:strict) if schema_def.is_a?(Hash)
255: 
256:       nil
257:     end
258: 
259:     def build_schema_payload(schema, schema_def, strict)
260:       {
261:         name: sanitize_schema_name(schema[:name] || 'response'),
262:         schema: schema_def,
263:         strict: strict.nil? || strict,
264:         description: schema[:description]
265:       }.compact
266:     end
267: 
268:     def sanitize_schema_name(name)
269:       sanitized = name.to_s.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/, '_')
270:       sanitized.empty? ? 'response' : sanitized
271:     end
272: 
273:     def add_callback(name, &block)
274:       @callbacks[name] << block if block
275:       self
276:     end
277: 
278:     def complete_once(&)
279:       response = provider_completion(&)
280: 
281:       run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message) unless block_given?
282: 
283:       normalize_schema_response(response)
284: 
285:       add_message response
286:       run_callbacks(:after_message, :end_message, response)
287: 
288:       if response.tool_call?
289:         handle_tool_calls(response, &)
290:       else
291:         response
292:       end
293:     end
294: 
295:     def provider_completion(&)
296:       @provider.complete(
297:         messages,
298:         tools: @tools,
299:         tool_prefs: @tool_prefs,
300:         temperature: @temperature,
301:         model: @model,
302:         params: @params,
303:         headers: @headers,
304:         schema: @schema,
305:         thinking: @thinking,
306:         citations: @citations,
307:         protocol: @protocol,
308:         &wrap_streaming_block(&)
309:       )
310:     end
311: 
312:     def normalize_schema_response(response)
313:       return unless @schema && response.content.is_a?(String) && !response.tool_call?
314: 
315:       response.content = JSON.parse(response.content)
316:     rescue JSON::ParserError
317:       # If parsing fails, keep content as string.
318:     end
319: 
320:     def set_legacy_callback(name, legacy_name, additive_name, &block)
321:       warn_legacy_callback_deprecation(legacy_name, additive_name) if block
322: 
323:       @on[name] = block
324:       self
325:     end
326: 
327:     def warn_legacy_callback_deprecation(legacy_name, additive_name)
328:       RubyLLM.deprecator.warn(
329:         "`#{legacy_name}` is deprecated and will be removed in RubyLLM 2.0. " \
330:         "Use `#{additive_name}` instead."
331:       )
332:     end
333: 
334:     def run_callbacks(name, legacy_name, *args)
335:       @callbacks[name].each { |callback| callback.call(*args) }
336:       @on[legacy_name]&.call(*args)
337:     end
338: 
339:     def wrap_streaming_block(&block)
340:       return nil unless block
341: 
342:       run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
343: 
344:       block
345:     end
346: 
347:     def handle_tool_calls(response, &)
348:       halt_result = if concurrency
349:                       handle_concurrent_tool_calls(response.tool_calls)
350:                     else
351:                       handle_sequential_tool_calls(response.tool_calls)
352:                     end
353: 
354:       reset_tool_choice if forced_tool_choice?
355:       halt_result || complete(&)
356:     end
357: 
358:     def handle_sequential_tool_calls(tool_calls)
359:       halt_result = nil
360: 
361:       tool_calls.each_value do |tool_call|
362:         run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
363:         result = execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
364:         add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
365:         halt_result = result if result.is_a?(Tool::Halt)
366:       end
367: 
368:       halt_result
369:     end
370: 
371:     def handle_concurrent_tool_calls(tool_calls)
372:       halt_result = nil
373: 
374:       execute_tools_concurrently(tool_calls) do |tool_call, result|
375:         run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
376:         add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
377:         halt_result = result if result.is_a?(Tool::Halt)
378:       end
379: 
380:       halt_result
381:     end
382: 
383:     def execute_tools_concurrently(tool_calls, &on_result)
384:       ToolConcurrency.run(concurrency, tool_calls, on_result:) do |tool_call|
385:         execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
386:       end
387:     end
388: 
389:     def execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
390:       run_callbacks(:before_tool_call, :tool_call, tool_call)
391:       result = execute_tool tool_call
392:       run_callbacks(:after_tool_result, :tool_result, result)
393:       result
394:     end
395: 
396:     def add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
397:       tool_payload = result.is_a?(Tool::Halt) ? result.content : result
398:       content = content_like?(tool_payload) ? tool_payload : tool_payload.to_s
399:       message = add_message role: :tool, content:, tool_call_id: tool_call.id
400:       run_callbacks(:after_message, :end_message, message)
401:       message
402:     end
403: 
404:     def execute_tool(tool_call)
405:       tool = tools[tool_call.name.to_sym]
406:       if tool.nil?
407:         return {
408:           error: "Model tried to call unavailable tool `#{tool_call.name}`. " \
409:                  "Available tools: #{tools.keys.to_json}."
410:         }
411:       end
412: 
413:       args = tool_call.arguments
414:       payload = {
415:         chat: self,
416:         provider: @provider.slug,
417:         provider_class: @provider.class.name,
418:         model: @model.id,
419:         model_info: @model,
420:         tool: tool,
421:         tool_call: tool_call,
422:         tool_name: tool.name,
423:         tool_arguments: args,
424:         tool_call_id: tool_call.id
425:       }
426: 
427:       RubyLLM.instrument('tool_call.ruby_llm', payload, config: @config) do |event|
428:         result = tool.call(args)
429:         event[:result] = result
430:         event[:result_content] = result.is_a?(Tool::Halt) ? result.content : result
431:         event[:result_class] = result.class.name
432:         result
433:       end
434:     end
435: 
436:     def update_tool_options(choice:, calls:)
437:       unless choice.nil?
438:         normalized_choice = normalize_tool_choice(choice)
439:         valid_tool_choices = %i[auto none required] + tools.keys
440:         unless valid_tool_choices.include?(normalized_choice)
441:           raise InvalidToolChoiceError,
442:                 "Invalid tool choice: #{choice}. Valid choices are: #{valid_tool_choices.join(', ')}"
443:         end
444: 
445:         @tool_prefs[:choice] = normalized_choice
446:       end
447: 
448:       @tool_prefs[:calls] = normalize_calls(calls) unless calls.nil?
449:     end
450: 
451:     def update_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
452:       @concurrency = normalize_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
453:     end
454: 
455:     def normalize_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
456:       return nil if concurrency.nil? || concurrency == false
457:       return :threads if concurrency == true
458: 
459:       normalized = concurrency.to_sym
460:       return normalized if ToolConcurrency::MODES.include?(normalized)
461: 
462:       raise ArgumentError,
463:             "Unknown tool concurrency: #{concurrency.inspect}. " \
464:             "Available modes: #{ToolConcurrency::MODES.join(', ')}"
465:     end
466: 
467:     def normalize_calls(calls)
468:       case calls
469:       when :many, 'many'
470:         :many
471:       when :one, 'one', 1
472:         :one
473:       else
474:         raise ArgumentError, "Invalid calls value: #{calls.inspect}. Valid values are: :many, :one, or 1"
475:       end
476:     end
477: 
478:     def normalize_tool_choice(choice)
479:       return choice.to_sym if choice.is_a?(String) || choice.is_a?(Symbol)
480:       return tool_name_for_choice_class(choice) if choice.is_a?(Class)
481: 
482:       choice.respond_to?(:name) ? choice.name.to_sym : choice.to_sym
483:     end
484: 
485:     def tool_name_for_choice_class(tool_class)
486:       matched_tool_name = tools.find { |_name, tool| tool.is_a?(tool_class) }&.first
487:       return matched_tool_name if matched_tool_name
488: 
489:       classify_tool_name(tool_class.name)
490:     end
491: 
492:     def classify_tool_name(class_name)
493:       Utils.underscore(class_name.split('::').last).delete_suffix('_tool').to_sym
494:     end
495: 
496:     def forced_tool_choice?
497:       @tool_prefs[:choice] && !%i[auto none].include?(@tool_prefs[:choice])
498:     end
499: 
500:     def reset_tool_choice
501:       @tool_prefs[:choice] = nil
502:     end
503: 
504:     def build_content(message, attachments)
505:       return message if content_like?(message)
506: 
507:       Content.new(message, attachments)
508:     end
509: 
510:     def content_like?(object)
511:       object.is_a?(Content) || object.is_a?(Content::Raw)
512:     end
513: 
514:     def append_system_instruction(instructions)
515:       system_messages, non_system_messages = @messages.partition { |msg| msg.role == :system }
516:       system_messages << Message.new(role: :system, content: instructions)
517:       @messages = system_messages + non_system_messages
518:     end
519: 
520:     def replace_system_instruction(instructions)
521:       system_messages, non_system_messages = @messages.partition { |msg| msg.role == :system }
522: 
523:       if system_messages.empty?
524:         system_messages = [Message.new(role: :system, content: instructions)]
525:       else
526:         system_messages.first.content = instructions
527:         system_messages = [system_messages.first]
528:       end
529: 
530:       @messages = system_messages + non_system_messages
531:     end
532:   end
533: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     # OpenRouter API integration.
6:     class OpenRouter < Provider
7:       # OpenRouter's dialect of the Chat Completions API.
8:       class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions
9:         include OpenRouter::Chat
10:         include OpenRouter::Images
11:         include OpenRouter::Models
12:         include OpenRouter::Streaming
13:       end
14: 
15:       protocol :chat_completions, ChatCompletions
16: 
17:       def api_base
18:         @config.openrouter_api_base || 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'
19:       end
20: 
21:       def headers
22:         {
23:           'Authorization' => "Bearer #{@config.openrouter_api_key}"
24:         }
25:       end
26: 
27:       def parse_error(response)
28:         return if response.body.empty?
29: 
30:         body = try_parse_json(response.body)
31:         case body
32:         when Hash
33:           parse_error_part_message body
34:         when Array
35:           body.map do |part|
36:             parse_error_part_message part
37:           end.join('. ')
38:         else
39:           body
40:         end
41:       end
42: 
43:       class << self
44:         def configuration_options
45:           %i[openrouter_api_key openrouter_api_base]
46:         end
47: 
48:         def configuration_requirements
49:           %i[openrouter_api_key]
50:         end
51:       end
52: 
53:       private
54: 
55:       def parse_error_part_message(part)
56:         message = part.dig('error', 'message')
57:         raw = try_parse_json(part.dig('error', 'metadata', 'raw'))
58:         return message unless raw.is_a?(Hash)
59: 
60:         raw_message = raw.dig('error', 'message')
61:         return [message, raw_message].compact.join(' - ') if raw_message
62: 
63:         message
64:       end
65:     end
66:   end
67: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     # Perplexity API integration.
6:     class Perplexity < Provider
7:       # Perplexity's dialect of the Chat Completions API.
8:       class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions
9:         include Perplexity::Chat
10:         include Perplexity::Models
11:       end
12: 
13:       protocol :chat_completions, ChatCompletions
14: 
15:       def api_base
16:         @config.perplexity_api_base || 'https://api.perplexity.ai'
17:       end
18: 
19:       def headers
20:         {
21:           'Authorization' => "Bearer #{@config.perplexity_api_key}",
22:           'Content-Type' => 'application/json'
23:         }
24:       end
25: 
26:       def parse_error(response)
27:         body = response.body
28:         return if body.empty?
29: 
30:         # If response is HTML (Perplexity returns HTML for auth errors)
31:         if body.include?('<html>') && body.include?('<title>')
32:           title_match = body.match(%r{<title>(.+?)</title>})
33:           if title_match
34:             message = title_match[1]
35:             message = message.sub(/^\d+\s+/, '')
36:             return message
37:           end
38:         end
39:         super
40:       end
41:

44:           Perplexity::Capabilities
45:         end
46:

48:           %i[perplexity_api_key perplexity_api_base]
49:         end
50: 
51:         def configuration_requirements
52:           %i[perplexity_api_key]
53:         end
54:       end
55:     end
56:   end
57: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'stringio'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   module Providers
7:     # Google Vertex AI implementation
8:     class VertexAI < Provider
9:       protocol :gemini, VertexAI::Gemini
10:       protocol :anthropic, VertexAI::Anthropic
11: 
12:       SCOPES = [
13:         'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform',
14:         'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/generative-language.retriever'
15:       ].freeze
16: 
17:       # Vertex AI hosts models from several publishers, each speaking its
18:       # native protocol.
19:       def protocol_for(model, **)
20:         model.id.start_with?('claude') ? protocols[:anthropic] : super
21:       end
22: 
23:       def model_path(model, publisher: 'google')
24:         "projects/#{@config.vertexai_project_id}/locations/#{@config.vertexai_location}" \
25:           "/publishers/#{publisher}/models/#{model}"
26:       end
27: 
28:       def initialize(config)
29:         super
30:         @authorizer = nil
31:       end
32: 
33:       def api_base
34:         return @config.vertexai_api_base if @config.vertexai_api_base
35: 
36:         if @config.vertexai_location.to_s == 'global'
37:           'https://aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1'
38:         else
39:           "https://#{@config.vertexai_location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1"
40:         end
41:       end
42: 
43:       def headers
44:         initialize_authorizer unless @authorizer
45:         @authorizer.apply({})
46:       rescue Google::Auth::AuthorizationError => e
47:         raise UnauthorizedError.new(nil, "Invalid Google Cloud credentials for Vertex AI: #{e.message}")
48:       end
49: 
50:       class << self
51:         def configuration_options
52:           %i[vertexai_project_id vertexai_location vertexai_service_account_key vertexai_api_base]
53:         end
54: 
55:         def configuration_requirements
56:           %i[vertexai_project_id vertexai_location]
57:         end
58:       end
59: 
60:       private
61: 
62:       def initialize_authorizer
63:         require 'googleauth'
64:         @authorizer =
65:           if @config.vertexai_service_account_key
66:             ::Google::Auth::ServiceAccountCredentials.make_creds(
67:               json_key_io: StringIO.new(@config.vertexai_service_account_key),
68:               scope: SCOPES
69:             )
70:           else
71:             ::Google::Auth.get_application_default(SCOPES)
72:           end
73:       rescue LoadError
74:         raise Error,
75:               'The googleauth gem ~> 1.15 is required for Vertex AI. Please add it to your Gemfile: gem "googleauth"'
76:       end
77:     end
78:   end
79: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'faraday'
4: require 'faraday/multipart'
5: require 'faraday/retry'
6: require 'ruby_llm/error_middleware'
7: require 'timeout'
8: 
9: module RubyLLM
10:   # Connection class for managing API connections to various providers.
11:   class Connection
12:     attr_reader :provider, :connection, :config
13: 
14:     def self.basic(&)
15:       Faraday.new do |f|
16:         f.response :logger,
17:                    RubyLLM.logger,
18:                    bodies: false,
19:                    errors: true,
20:                    headers: false,
21:                    log_level: :debug
22:         f.response :raise_error
23:         yield f if block_given?
24:       end
25:     end
26: 
27:     def initialize(provider, config)
28:       @provider = provider
29:       @config = config
30: 
31:       @connection = Faraday.new(provider.api_base) do |faraday|
32:         setup_timeout(faraday)
33:         setup_logging(faraday)
34:         setup_retry(faraday)
35:         setup_middleware(faraday)
36:         setup_http_proxy(faraday)
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     def post(url, payload, &)
41:       instrument_request(:post, url) do
42:         @connection.post url, payload do |req|
43:           req.headers.merge! @provider.headers
44:           yield req if block_given?
45:         end
46:       end
47:     end
48: 
49:     def get(url, &)
50:       instrument_request(:get, url) do
51:         @connection.get url do |req|
52:           req.headers.merge! @provider.headers
53:           yield req if block_given?
54:         end
55:       end
56:     end
57: 
58:     # Keeps the config and Faraday internals out of pretty-printed output.
59:     def pretty_print_instance_variables
60:       super - %i[@config @connection]
61:     end
62: 
63:     private
64: 
65:     def instrument_request(method, url)
66:       payload = {
67:         provider: @provider.slug,
68:         method: method,
69:         url: url
70:       }
71: 
72:       RubyLLM.instrument('request.ruby_llm', payload, config: @config) do
73:         response = yield
74:         payload[:status] = response.status if response.respond_to?(:status)
75:         response
76:       end
77:     end
78: 
79:     def setup_timeout(faraday)
80:       faraday.options.timeout = @config.request_timeout
81:     end
82: 
83:     def setup_logging(faraday)
84:       faraday.response :logger,
85:                        RubyLLM.logger,
86:                        bodies: RubyLLM.logger.debug?,
87:                        errors: true,
88:                        headers: false,
89:                        log_level: :debug do |logger|
90:         logger.filter(logging_regexp('[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{100,}'), '[BASE64 DATA]')
91:         logger.filter(logging_regexp('[-\\d.e,\\s]{100,}'), '[EMBEDDINGS ARRAY]')
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     def logging_regexp(pattern)
96:       return Regexp.new(pattern) if @config.log_regexp_timeout.nil? || !Regexp.respond_to?(:timeout)
97: 
98:       Regexp.new(pattern, timeout: @config.log_regexp_timeout)
99:     end
100: 
101:     def setup_retry(faraday)
102:       faraday.request :retry, {
103:         max: @config.max_retries,
104:         interval: @config.retry_interval,
105:         interval_randomness: @config.retry_interval_randomness,
106:         backoff_factor: @config.retry_backoff_factor,
107:         methods: Faraday::Retry::Middleware::IDEMPOTENT_METHODS + [:post],
108:         exceptions: retry_exceptions
109:       }
110:     end
111: 
112:     def setup_middleware(faraday)
113:       faraday.request :multipart
114:       faraday.request :json
115:       faraday.response :json
116:       faraday.adapter(@config.faraday_adapter)
117:       faraday.use :llm_errors, provider: @provider
118:     end
119: 
120:     def setup_http_proxy(faraday)
121:       return unless @config.http_proxy
122: 
123:       faraday.proxy = @config.http_proxy
124:     end
125: 
126:     def retry_exceptions
127:       [
128:         Errno::ETIMEDOUT,
129:         Timeout::Error,
130:         Faraday::TimeoutError,
131:         Faraday::ConnectionFailed,
132:         Faraday::RetriableResponse,
133:         RubyLLM::RateLimitError,
134:         RubyLLM::ServerError,
135:         RubyLLM::ServiceUnavailableError,
136:         RubyLLM::OverloadedError
137:       ]
138:     end
139:   end
140: end

(End of file - total 140 lines)
