<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/ruby_llm/lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/chat.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Chat methods of the OpenAI API integration
7:       module Chat
8:         def completion_url
9:           'chat/completions'
10:         end
11: 
12:         module_function
13: 
14:         # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists,Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
15:         def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false, schema: nil,
16:                            thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
17:           warn_unsupported_citations(model) if citations && !model.citations?
18:           tool_prefs ||= {}
19:           payload = {
20:             model: model.id,
21:             messages: format_messages(messages),
22:             stream: stream
23:           }
24: 
25:           payload[:temperature] = temperature unless temperature.nil?
26:           if tools.any?
27:             payload[:tools] = tools.map { |_, tool| tool_for(tool) }
28:             payload[:tool_choice] = build_tool_choice(tool_prefs[:choice]) unless tool_prefs[:choice].nil?
29:             payload[:parallel_tool_calls] = tool_prefs[:calls] == :many unless tool_prefs[:calls].nil?
30:           end
31: 
32:           if schema
33:             schema_name = schema[:name]
34:             schema_def = schema[:schema]
35:             strict = schema[:strict]
36: 
37:             payload[:response_format] = {
38:               type: 'json_schema',
39:               json_schema: {
40:                 name: schema_name,
41:                 schema: schema_def,
42:                 strict: strict
43:               }
44:             }
45:           end
46: 
47:           effort = resolve_effort(thinking)
48:           payload[:reasoning_effort] = effort if effort
49: 
50:           payload[:stream_options] = { include_usage: true } if stream
51:           payload
52:         end
53:         # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists,Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
54: 
55:         def warn_unsupported_citations(model)
56:           RubyLLM.logger.warn(
57:             "#{model.id} does not support citations according to the model registry. " \
58:             'with_citations may have no effect.'
59:           )
60:         end
61: 
62:         def parse_completion_response(response)
63:           data = response.body
64:           return if data.nil? || data.empty?
65: 
66:           raise Error.new(response, data.dig('error', 'message')) if data.dig('error', 'message')
67: 
68:           message_data = data.dig('choices', 0, 'message')
69:           return unless message_data
70: 
71:           usage = data['usage'] || {}
72:           thinking_tokens = thinking_tokens(usage)
73:           content, thinking_from_blocks = extract_content_and_thinking(message_data['content'])
74:           thinking_text = thinking_from_blocks || extract_thinking_text(message_data)
75:           thinking_signature = extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
76: 
77:           Message.new(
78:             role: :assistant,
79:             content: content,
80:             citations: extract_citations(message_data, data, content),
81:             thinking: Thinking.build(text: thinking_text, signature: thinking_signature),
82:             tool_calls: parse_tool_calls(message_data['tool_calls']),
83:             input_tokens: input_tokens(usage),
84:             output_tokens: output_tokens(usage),
85:             cached_tokens: cache_read_tokens(usage),
86:             cache_creation_tokens: cache_write_tokens(usage),
87:             thinking_tokens: thinking_tokens,
88:             model_id: data['model'],
89:             raw: response
90:           )
91:         end
92: 
93:         def input_tokens(usage)
94:           return usage['prompt_cache_miss_tokens'] if usage['prompt_cache_miss_tokens']
95: 
96:           prompt_tokens = usage['prompt_tokens']
97:           return unless prompt_tokens
98: 
99:           [prompt_tokens.to_i - cache_read_tokens(usage).to_i - cache_write_tokens(usage).to_i, 0].max
100:         end
101: 
102:         def output_tokens(usage)
103:           completion_tokens = usage['completion_tokens']
104:           return unless completion_tokens
105: 
106:           completion_tokens = completion_tokens.to_i
107:           generated_tokens = generated_tokens_from_total(usage)
108:           return completion_tokens unless generated_tokens && generated_tokens > completion_tokens
109: 
110:           generated_tokens
111:         end
112: 
113:         def generated_tokens_from_total(usage)
114:           prompt_tokens = usage['prompt_tokens']
115:           total_tokens = usage['total_tokens']
116:           return unless prompt_tokens && total_tokens
117: 
118:           [total_tokens.to_i - prompt_tokens.to_i, 0].max
119:         end
120: 
121:         def cache_read_tokens(usage)
122:           usage.dig('prompt_tokens_details', 'cached_tokens') || usage['prompt_cache_hit_tokens']
123:         end
124: 
125:         def cache_write_tokens(usage)
126:           usage.dig('prompt_tokens_details', 'cache_write_tokens') || 0
127:         end
128: 
129:         def thinking_tokens(usage)
130:           usage.dig('completion_tokens_details', 'reasoning_tokens') || usage['reasoning_tokens']
131:         end
132: 
133:         def extract_citations(message_data, data, content)
134:           annotations = parse_annotations(message_data['annotations'], content)
135:           return annotations if annotations.any?
136: 
137:           parse_root_citations(data)
138:         end
139: 
140:         def parse_annotations(annotations, content)
141:           Array(annotations).filter_map do |annotation|
142:             details = annotation['url_citation']
143:             next unless details.is_a?(Hash)
144: 
145:             start_index = details['start_index']
146:             end_index = details['end_index']
147: 
148:             Citation.new(
149:               url: details['url'],
150:               title: details['title'],
151:               text: annotated_text(content, start_index, end_index),
152:               start_index: start_index,
153:               end_index: end_index
154:             )
155:           end
156:         end
157: 
158:         def annotated_text(content, start_index, end_index)
159:           return nil unless content.is_a?(String) && start_index && end_index
160: 
161:           content[start_index...end_index]
162:         end
163: 
164:         # Perplexity and xAI return search citations at the root of the response.
165:         def parse_root_citations(data)
166:           search_results = data['search_results']
167:           return parse_search_results(search_results) if search_results.is_a?(Array) && search_results.any?
168: 
169:           Array(data['citations']).each_with_index.filter_map do |url, index|
170:             Citation.new(url: url, source_index: index) if url.is_a?(String)
171:           end
172:         end
173: 
174:         def parse_search_results(results)
175:           results.each_with_index.filter_map do |result, index|
176:             next unless result.is_a?(Hash)
177: 
178:             Citation.new(
179:               url: result['url'],
180:               title: result['title'],
181:               cited_text: result['snippet'],
182:               source_index: index
183:             )
184:           end
185:         end
186: 
187:         def format_messages(messages)
188:           messages.map do |msg|
189:             {
190:               role: format_role(msg.role),
191:               content: format_message_content(msg),
192:               tool_calls: format_tool_calls(msg.tool_calls),
193:               tool_call_id: msg.tool_call_id
194:             }.compact.merge(format_thinking(msg))
195:           end
196:         end
197: 
198:         def format_message_content(msg)
199:           content = format_content(msg.content)
200:           return '' if content.nil? && thinking_only_assistant_message?(msg)
201: 
202:           content
203:         end
204: 
205:         def thinking_only_assistant_message?(msg)
206:           msg.role == :assistant && msg.thinking && !msg.tool_call?
207:         end
208: 
209:         def format_content(content)
210:           Media.format_content(content)
211:         end
212: 
213:         def format_role(role)
214:           case role
215:           when :system
216:             @config.openai_use_system_role ? 'system' : 'developer'
217:           else
218:             role.to_s
219:           end
220:         end
221: 
222:         def resolve_effort(thinking)
223:           return nil unless thinking
224: 
225:           thinking.respond_to?(:effort) ? thinking.effort : thinking
226:         end
227: 
228:         def format_thinking(msg)
229:           return {} unless msg.role == :assistant
230: 
231:           thinking = msg.thinking
232:           return {} unless thinking
233: 
234:           payload = {}
235:           if thinking.text
236:             payload[:reasoning] = thinking.text
237:             payload[:reasoning_content] = thinking.text
238:           end
239:           payload[:reasoning_signature] = thinking.signature if thinking.signature
240:           payload
241:         end
242: 
243:         def extract_thinking_text(message_data)
244:           candidate = message_data['reasoning_content'] || message_data['reasoning'] || message_data['thinking']
245:           candidate.is_a?(String) ? candidate : nil
246:         end
247: 
248:         def extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
249:           candidate = message_data['reasoning_signature'] || message_data['signature']
250:           candidate.is_a?(String) ? candidate : nil
251:         end
252: 
253:         def extract_content_and_thinking(content)
254:           return extract_think_tag_content(content) if content.is_a?(String)
255:           return [content, nil] unless content.is_a?(Array)
256: 
257:           text = extract_text_from_blocks(content)
258:           thinking = extract_thinking_from_blocks(content)
259: 
260:           [text.empty? ? nil : text, thinking.empty? ? nil : thinking]
261:         end
262: 
263:         def extract_text_from_blocks(blocks)
264:           blocks.filter_map do |block|
265:             block['text'] if block['type'] == 'text' && block['text'].is_a?(String)
266:           end.join
267:         end
268: 
269:         def extract_thinking_from_blocks(blocks)
270:           blocks.filter_map do |block|
271:             next unless block['type'] == 'thinking'
272: 
273:             extract_thinking_text_from_block(block)
274:           end.join
275:         end
276: 
277:         def extract_thinking_text_from_block(block)
278:           thinking_block = block['thinking']
279:           return thinking_block if thinking_block.is_a?(String)
280: 
281:           if thinking_block.is_a?(Array)
282:             return thinking_block.filter_map { |item| item['text'] if item['type'] == 'text' }.join
283:           end
284: 
285:           block['text'] if block['text'].is_a?(String)
286:         end
287: 
288:         def extract_think_tag_content(text)
289:           return [text, nil] unless text.include?('<think>')
290: 
291:           thinking = text.scan(%r{<think>(.*?)</think>}m).join
292:           content = text.gsub(%r{<think>.*?</think>}m, '').strip
293: 
294:           [content.empty? ? nil : content, thinking.empty? ? nil : thinking]
295:         end
296:       end
297:     end
298:   end
299: end

(End of file - total 299 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'json'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   module Protocols
7:     class ChatCompletions
8:       # Streaming methods of the OpenAI API integration
9:       module Streaming
10:         module_function
11: 
12:         def stream_url
13:           completion_url
14:         end
15: 
16:         def build_chunk(data)
17:           usage = data['usage'] || {}
18:           delta = data.dig('choices', 0, 'delta') || {}
19:           content_source = delta['content'] || data.dig('choices', 0, 'message', 'content')
20:           content, thinking_from_blocks = extract_content_and_thinking(content_source)
21: 
22:           Chunk.new(
23:             role: :assistant,
24:             model_id: data['model'],
25:             content: content,
26:             citations: extract_chunk_citations(delta, data),
27:             thinking: Thinking.build(
28:               text: thinking_from_blocks || delta['reasoning_content'] || delta['reasoning'],
29:               signature: delta['reasoning_signature']
30:             ),
31:             tool_calls: parse_tool_calls(delta['tool_calls'], parse_arguments: false),
32:             input_tokens: input_tokens(usage),
33:             output_tokens: output_tokens(usage),
34:             cached_tokens: cache_read_tokens(usage),
35:             cache_creation_tokens: cache_write_tokens(usage),
36:             thinking_tokens: thinking_tokens(usage)
37:           )
38:         end
39: 
40:         def extract_chunk_citations(delta, data)
41:           annotations = parse_annotations(delta['annotations'], nil)
42:           return annotations if annotations.any?
43: 
44:           parse_root_citations(data)
45:         end
46: 
47:         def parse_streaming_error(data)
48:           error_data = JSON.parse(data)
49:           return unless error_data['error']
50: 
51:           case error_data.dig('error', 'type')
52:           when 'server_error'
53:             [500, error_data['error']['message']]
54:           when 'rate_limit_exceeded', 'insufficient_quota'
55:             [429, error_data['error']['message']]
56:           else
57:             [400, error_data['error']['message']]
58:           end
59:         end
60:       end
61:     end
62:   end
63: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Handles formatting of media content (images, audio) for OpenAI APIs
7:       module Media
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def format_content(content, document_attachments: :pdf, image_attachments: true, audio_attachments: true)
11:           format_parts(content) do |attachment|
12:             format_attachment(
13:               attachment,
14:               document_attachments:,
15:               image_attachments:,
16:               audio_attachments:
17:             )
18:           end
19:         end
20: 
21:         # Shared preamble and attachment loop for OpenAI-compatible providers.
22:         # The block formats a single attachment in the provider's dialect.
23:         def format_parts(content)
24:           if content.is_a?(RubyLLM::Content::Raw)
25:             value = content.value
26:             return value.is_a?(Hash) ? value.to_json : value
27:           end
28:           return content.to_json if content.is_a?(Hash) || content.is_a?(Array)
29:           return content unless content.is_a?(Content)
30: 
31:           parts = []
32:           parts << format_text(content.text) if content.text
33: 
34:           content.attachments.each do |attachment|
35:             parts << yield(attachment)
36:           end
37: 
38:           parts
39:         end
40: 
41:         def format_attachment(attachment, document_attachments:, image_attachments:, audio_attachments:)
42:           case attachment.type
43:           when :image
44:             raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless image_attachments
45: 
46:             format_image(attachment)
47:           when :audio
48:             raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless audio_attachments
49: 
50:             format_audio(attachment)
51:           when :pdf, :document
52:             format_document_attachment(attachment, document_attachments)
53:           when :text
54:             format_text_file(attachment)
55:           else
56:             raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
57:           end
58:         end
59: 
60:         def format_image(image)
61:           {
62:             type: 'image_url',
63:             image_url: {
64:               url: image.url? ? image.source.to_s : image.for_llm
65:             }
66:           }
67:         end
68: 
69:         def format_document(document)
70:           {
71:             type: 'file',
72:             file: {
73:               filename: document.filename,
74:               file_data: document.for_llm
75:             }
76:           }
77:         end
78: 
79:         def format_text_file(text_file)
80:           {
81:             type: 'text',
82:             text: text_file.for_llm
83:           }
84:         end
85: 
86:         def format_audio(audio)
87:           {
88:             type: 'input_audio',
89:             input_audio: {
90:               data: audio.encoded,
91:               format: audio.format
92:             }
93:           }
94:         end
95: 
96:         def format_text(text)
97:           {
98:             type: 'text',
99:             text: text
100:           }
101:         end
102: 
103:         def format_document_attachment(attachment, strategy)
104:           return format_document(attachment) if strategy == :all
105:           return format_document(attachment) if strategy == :pdf && attachment.pdf?
106: 
107:           raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
108:         end
109:       end
110:     end
111:   end
112: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'json'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   module Protocols
7:     class ChatCompletions
8:       # Tools methods of the OpenAI API integration
9:       module Tools
10:         module_function
11: 
12:         EMPTY_PARAMETERS_SCHEMA = {
13:           'type' => 'object',
14:           'properties' => {},
15:           'required' => [],
16:           'additionalProperties' => false,
17:           'strict' => true
18:         }.freeze
19: 
20:         def parameters_schema_for(tool)
21:           tool.params_schema ||
22:             schema_from_parameters(tool.parameters)
23:         end
24: 
25:         def schema_from_parameters(parameters)
26:           schema_definition = RubyLLM::Tool::SchemaDefinition.from_parameters(parameters)
27:           schema_definition&.json_schema || EMPTY_PARAMETERS_SCHEMA
28:         end
29: 
30:         def tool_for(tool)
31:           parameters_schema = parameters_schema_for(tool)
32: 
33:           definition = {
34:             type: 'function',
35:             function: {
36:               name: tool.name,
37:               description: tool.description,
38:               parameters: parameters_schema
39:             }
40:           }
41: 
42:           return definition if tool.provider_params.empty?
43: 
44:           RubyLLM::Utils.deep_merge(definition, tool.provider_params)
45:         end
46: 
47:         def format_tool_calls(tool_calls)
48:           return nil unless tool_calls&.any?
49: 
50:           tool_calls.map do |_, tc|
51:             call = {
52:               id: tc.id,
53:               type: 'function',
54:               function: {
55:                 name: tc.name,
56:                 arguments: JSON.generate(tc.arguments)
57:               }
58:             }
59:             if tc.thought_signature
60:               call[:extra_content] = {
61:                 google: { thought_signature: tc.thought_signature }
62:               }
63:             end
64:             call
65:           end
66:         end
67: 
68:         def parse_tool_call_arguments(tool_call)
69:           arguments = tool_call.dig('function', 'arguments')
70: 
71:           if arguments.nil? || arguments.empty?
72:             {}
73:           else
74:             JSON.parse(arguments)
75:           end
76:         end
77: 
78:         def parse_tool_calls(tool_calls, parse_arguments: true)
79:           return nil unless tool_calls&.any?
80: 
81:           tool_calls.to_h do |tc|
82:             [
83:               tc['id'],
84:               ToolCall.new(
85:                 id: tc['id'],
86:                 name: tc.dig('function', 'name'),
87:                 arguments: if parse_arguments
88:                              parse_tool_call_arguments(tc)
89:                            else
90:                              tc.dig('function', 'arguments')
91:                            end,
92:                 thought_signature: extract_tool_call_thought_signature(tc)
93:               )
94:             ]
95:           end
96:         end
97: 
98:         def build_tool_choice(tool_choice)
99:           case tool_choice
100:           when :auto, :none, :required
101:             tool_choice
102:           else
103:             {
104:               type: 'function',
105:               function: {
106:                 name: tool_choice
107:               }
108:             }
109:           end
110:         end
111: 
112:         def extract_tool_call_thought_signature(tool_call)
113:           tool_call.dig('extra_content', 'google', 'thought_signature')
114:         end
115:       end
116:     end
117:   end
118: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'faraday'
4: require 'ruby_llm/error'
5: 
6: module RubyLLM
7:   # Faraday middleware that maps provider-specific API errors to RubyLLM errors.
8:   class ErrorMiddleware < Faraday::Middleware
9:     def initialize(app, options = {})
10:       super(app)
11:       @provider = options[:provider]
12:     end
13: 
14:     def call(env)
15:       @app.call(env).on_complete do |response|
16:         self.class.parse_error(provider: @provider, response: response)
17:       end
18:     end
19: 
20:     class << self
21:       CONTEXT_LENGTH_PATTERNS = [
22:         /context length/i,
23:         /context window/i,
24:         /maximum context/i,
25:         /request too large/i,
26:         /too many tokens/i,
27:         /token count exceeds/i,
28:         /input[_\s-]?token/i,
29:         /input or output tokens? must be reduced/i,
30:         /reduce the length of messages/i,
31:         /prompt is too long/i
32:       ].freeze
33: 
34:       RATE_LIMIT_PATTERNS = [
35:         /rate limit/i,
36:         /per minute/i,
37:         /per hour/i,
38:         /per day/i
39:       ].freeze
40: 
41:       def parse_error(provider:, response:)
42:         message = provider&.parse_error(response)
43: 
44:         case response.status
45:         when 200..399
46:           message
47:         when 400
48:           raise ContextLengthExceededError.new(response, message) if context_length_exceeded?(message)
49: 
50:           raise BadRequestError.new(response, message)
51:         when 401
52:           raise UnauthorizedError.new(response, message)
53:         when 402
54:           raise PaymentRequiredError.new(response, message)
55:         when 403
56:           raise ForbiddenError.new(response, message)
57:         when 429
58:           raise RateLimitError.new(response, message) if rate_limited?(message)
59:           raise ContextLengthExceededError.new(response, message) if context_length_exceeded?(message)
60: 
61:           raise RateLimitError.new(response, message)
62:         when 500
63:           raise ServerError.new(response, message)
64:         when 502..504
65:           raise ServiceUnavailableError.new(response, message)
66:         when 529
67:           raise OverloadedError.new(response, message)
68:         else
69:           raise Error.new(response, message)
70:         end
71:       end
72: 
73:       private
74: 
75:       def context_length_exceeded?(message)
76:         return false if message.to_s.empty?
77: 
78:         CONTEXT_LENGTH_PATTERNS.any? { |pattern| message.match?(pattern) }
79:       end
80: 
81:       def rate_limited?(message)
82:         return false if message.to_s.empty?
83: 
84:         RATE_LIMIT_PATTERNS.any? { |pattern| message.match?(pattern) }
85:       end
86:     end
87:   end
88: end
89: 
90: Faraday::Middleware.register_middleware(llm_errors: RubyLLM::ErrorMiddleware)

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'faraday'
4: require 'stringio'
5: 
6: module RubyLLM
7:   module Protocols
8:     class ChatCompletions
9:       # Image generation methods for the OpenAI API integration
10:       module Images
11:         module_function
12: 
13:         def images_url(with: nil, mask: nil)
14:           editing?(with, mask) ? 'images/edits' : 'images/generations'
15:         end
16: 
17:         def render_image_payload(prompt, model:, size:, with: nil, mask: nil, params: {}) # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
18:           return render_edit_payload(prompt, model:, with:, mask:, params:) if editing?(with, mask)
19: 
20:           {
21:             model: model,
22:             prompt: prompt,
23:             n: 1,
24:             size: size
25:           }.merge(params)
26:         end
27: 
28:         def parse_image_response(response, model:)
29:           data = response.body
30:           image_data = Array(data['data']).first
31: 
32:           raise Error.new(nil, 'Unexpected response format from OpenAI image API') unless image_data
33: 
34:           Image.new(
35:             url: image_data['url'],
36:             mime_type: 'image/png', # DALL-E typically returns PNGs
37:             revised_prompt: image_data['revised_prompt'],
38:             model_id: model,
39:             data: image_data['b64_json'],
40:             usage: data['usage'] || {}
41:           )
42:         end
43: 
44:         def validate_paint_inputs!(with:, mask:)
45:           return unless editing?(with, mask)
46: 
47:           raise ArgumentError, 'with: is required when mask: is provided' if mask && !attachments?(with)
48:         end
49: 
50:         def render_edit_payload(prompt, model:, with:, mask:, params:)
51:           payload = params.merge(
52:             model: model,
53:             prompt: prompt,
54:             image: build_upload_parts(with),
55:             n: 1
56:           )
57:           payload[:mask] = build_upload_part(mask) if mask
58:           payload
59:         end
60: 
61:         def build_upload_parts(sources)
62:           Array(sources).filter_map do |source|
63:             next if blank_attachment?(source)
64: 
65:             build_upload_part(source)
66:           end
67:         end
68: 
69:         def build_upload_part(source)
70:           attachment = Attachment.new(source)
71:           raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless attachment.image?
72: 
73:           Faraday::UploadIO.new(StringIO.new(attachment.content), attachment.mime_type, attachment.filename)
74:         end
75: 
76:         def editing?(with, mask)
77:           attachments?(with) || !mask.nil?
78:         end
79: 
80:         def attachments?(value)
81:           Array(value).any? { |item| !blank_attachment?(item) }
82:         end
83: 
84:         def blank_attachment?(value)
85:           value.nil? || (value.is_a?(String) && value.strip.empty?)
86:         end
87:       end
88:     end
89:   end
90: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Audio transcription methods for the OpenAI API integration
7:       module Transcription
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def transcription_url
11:           'audio/transcriptions'
12:         end
13: 
14:         def render_transcription_payload(file_part, model:, language:, **options)
15:           {
16:             model: model,
17:             file: file_part,
18:             language: language,
19:             chunking_strategy: (options[:chunking_strategy] || 'auto' if supports_chunking_strategy?(model, options)),
20:             response_format: response_format_for(model, options),
21:             prompt: options[:prompt],
22:             temperature: options[:temperature],
23:             timestamp_granularities: options[:timestamp_granularities],
24:             known_speaker_names: options[:speaker_names],
25:             known_speaker_references: encode_speaker_references(options[:speaker_references])
26:           }.compact
27:         end
28: 
29:         def encode_speaker_references(references)
30:           return nil unless references
31: 
32:           references.map do |ref|
33:             Attachment.new(ref).for_llm
34:           end
35:         end
36: 
37:         def response_format_for(model, options)
38:           return options[:response_format] if options.key?(:response_format)
39: 
40:           'diarized_json' if model.include?('diarize')
41:         end
42: 
43:         def supports_chunking_strategy?(model, options)
44:           return false if model.start_with?('whisper')
45:           return true if options.key?(:chunking_strategy)
46: 
47:           model.include?('diarize')
48:         end
49: 
50:         def parse_transcription_response(response, model:)
51:           data = response.body
52: 
53:           return RubyLLM::Transcription.new(text: data, model: model) if data.is_a?(String)
54: 
55:           usage = data['usage'] || {}
56: 
57:           RubyLLM::Transcription.new(
58:             text: data['text'],
59:             model: model,
60:             language: data['language'],
61:             duration: data['duration'],
62:             segments: data['segments'],
63:             words: data['words'],
64:             input_tokens: usage['input_tokens'] || usage['prompt_tokens'],
65:             output_tokens: usage['output_tokens'] || usage['completion_tokens']
66:           )
67:         end
68:       end
69:     end
70:   end
71: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Normalizes temperature for OpenAI models with provider-specific requirements.
7:       module Temperature
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def normalize(temperature, model_id)
11:           if forced_temperature_model?(model_id) && !temperature.nil? && !temperature_close_to_one?(temperature)
12:             RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Model #{model_id} requires temperature=1.0, setting that instead." }
13:             1.0
14:           elsif model_id.include?('-search')
15:             RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Model #{model_id} does not accept temperature parameter, removing" }
16:             nil
17:           else
18:             temperature
19:           end
20:         end
21: 
22:         def temperature_close_to_one?(temperature)
23:           (temperature.to_f - 1.0).abs <= Float::EPSILON
24:         end
25: 
26:         def forced_temperature_model?(model_id)
27:           model_id.match?(/^o\d/) ||                          # o1, o3, o4-mini, etc.
28:             model_id.match?(/^gpt-5(\.\d+)?(-\d{4})?$/) ||    # gpt-5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-2026-03-05
29:             model_id.match?(/^gpt-5(\.\d+)?-pro/)             # gpt-5-pro, gpt-5.4-pro
30:         end
31:       end
32:     end
33:   end
34: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class OpenRouter
6:       # Chat methods of the OpenRouter API integration
7:       module Chat
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
11:         def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false, schema: nil,
12:                            thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
13:           payload = super
14:           payload.delete(:reasoning_effort)
15:           strip_schema_strict(payload)
16: 
17:           reasoning = build_reasoning(thinking)
18:           payload[:reasoning] = reasoning if reasoning
19:           payload
20:         end
21:         # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
22: 
23:         def strip_schema_strict(payload)
24:           schema_def = payload.dig(:response_format, :json_schema, :schema)
25:           return unless schema_def.is_a?(Hash)
26: 
27:           schema_def = RubyLLM::Utils.deep_dup(schema_def)
28:           schema_def.delete(:strict)
29:           schema_def.delete('strict')
30:           payload[:response_format][:json_schema][:schema] = schema_def
31:         end
32: 
33:         def build_reasoning(thinking)
34:           return nil unless thinking&.enabled?
35: 
36:           reasoning = {}
37:           reasoning[:effort] = thinking.effort if thinking.respond_to?(:effort) && thinking.effort
38:           reasoning[:max_tokens] = thinking.budget if thinking.respond_to?(:budget) && thinking.budget
39:           reasoning[:enabled] = true if reasoning.empty?
40:           reasoning
41:         end
42: 
43:         def format_thinking(msg)
44:           thinking = msg.thinking
45:           return {} unless thinking && msg.role == :assistant
46: 
47:           details = []
48:           if thinking.text
49:             details << {
50:               type: 'reasoning.text',
51:               text: thinking.text,
52:               signature: thinking.signature
53:             }.compact
54:           elsif thinking.signature
55:             details << {
56:               type: 'reasoning.encrypted',
57:               data: thinking.signature
58:             }
59:           end
60: 
61:           details.empty? ? {} : { reasoning_details: details }
62:         end
63: 
64:         def extract_thinking_text(message_data)
65:           candidate = message_data['reasoning']
66:           return candidate if candidate.is_a?(String)
67: 
68:           details = message_data['reasoning_details']
69:           return nil unless details.is_a?(Array)
70: 
71:           text = details.filter_map do |detail|
72:             case detail['type']
73:             when 'reasoning.text'
74:               detail['text']
75:             when 'reasoning.summary'
76:               detail['summary']
77:             end
78:           end.join
79: 
80:           text.empty? ? nil : text
81:         end
82: 
83:         def extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
84:           details = message_data['reasoning_details']
85:           return nil unless details.is_a?(Array)
86: 
87:           signature = details.filter_map do |detail|
88:             detail['signature'] if detail['signature'].is_a?(String)
89:           end.first
90:           return signature if signature
91: 
92:           encrypted = details.find { |detail| detail['type'] == 'reasoning.encrypted' && detail['data'].is_a?(String) }
93:           encrypted&.dig('data')
94:         end
95:       end
96:     end
97:   end
98: end

(End of file - total 98 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class OpenRouter
6:       # Models methods of the OpenRouter API integration
7:       module Models
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def models_url
11:           'models'
12:         end
13: 
14:         def parse_list_models_response(response, slug, _capabilities)
15:           Array(response.body['data']).map do |model_data| # rubocop:disable Metrics/BlockLength
16:             modalities = {
17:               input: Array(model_data.dig('architecture', 'input_modalities')),
18:               output: Array(model_data.dig('architecture', 'output_modalities'))
19:             }
20: 
21:             pricing = { text_tokens: { standard: {} } }
22: 
23:             pricing_types = {
24:               prompt: :input_per_million,
25:               completion: :output_per_million,
26:               input_cache_read: :cache_read_input_per_million,
27:               internal_reasoning: :reasoning_output_per_million
28:             }
29: 
30:             pricing_types.each do |source_key, target_key|
31:               value = model_data.dig('pricing', source_key.to_s).to_f
32:               pricing[:text_tokens][:standard][target_key] = value * 1_000_000 if value.positive?
33:             end
34: 
35:             capabilities = supported_parameters_to_capabilities(model_data['supported_parameters'])
36: 
37:             Model::Info.new(
38:               id: model_data['id'],
39:               name: model_data['name'],
40:               provider: slug,
41:               family: model_data['id'].split('/').first,
42:               created_at: model_data['created'] ? Time.at(model_data['created']) : nil,
43:               context_window: model_data['context_length'],
44:               max_output_tokens: model_data.dig('top_provider', 'max_completion_tokens'),
45:               modalities: modalities,
46:               capabilities: capabilities,
47:               pricing: pricing,
48:               metadata: {
49:                 description: model_data['description'],
50:                 architecture: model_data['architecture'],
51:                 top_provider: model_data['top_provider'],
52:                 per_request_limits: model_data['per_request_limits'],
53:                 supported_parameters: model_data['supported_parameters']
54:               }
55:             )
56:           end
57:         end
58: 
59:         def supported_parameters_to_capabilities(params)
60:           return [] unless params
61: 
62:           capabilities = []
63:           capabilities << 'streaming'
64:           capabilities << 'function_calling' if params.include?('tools') || params.include?('tool_choice')
65:           capabilities << 'structured_output' if params.include?('response_format')
66:           capabilities << 'batch' if params.include?('batch')
67:           capabilities << 'predicted_outputs' if params.include?('logit_bias') && params.include?('top_k')
68:           capabilities
69:         end
70:       end
71:     end
72:   end
73: end

23:             input_tokens: input_tokens(usage),
24:             output_tokens: output_tokens(usage),
25:             cached_tokens: cache_read_tokens(usage),
26:             cache_creation_tokens: cache_write_tokens(usage),

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class OpenRouter
6:       # Image generation methods for the OpenRouter API integration.
7:       # OpenRouter uses the chat completions endpoint for image generation
8:       # instead of a dedicated images endpoint.
9:       module Images
10:         module_function
11: 
12:         def images_url(with: nil, mask: nil) # rubocop:disable Lint/UnusedMethodArgument
13:           'chat/completions'
14:         end
15: 
16:         def render_image_payload(prompt, model:, size:, with: nil, mask: nil, params: {}) # rubocop:disable Lint/UnusedMethodArgument,Metrics/ParameterLists
17:           RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Ignoring size #{size}. OpenRouter image generation does not support size parameter." }
18:           {
19:             model: model,
20:             messages: [
21:               {
22:                 role: 'user',
23:                 content: prompt
24:               }
25:             ],
26:             modalities: %w[image text]
27:           }
28:         end
29: 
30:         def parse_image_response(response, model:)
31:           data = response.body
32:           message = data.dig('choices', 0, 'message')
33: 
34:           unless message&.key?('images') && message['images']&.any?
35:             raise Error.new(nil, 'Unexpected response format from OpenRouter image generation API')
36:           end
37: 
38:           image_data = message['images'].first
39:           image_url = image_data.dig('image_url', 'url') || image_data['url']
40: 
41:           raise Error.new(nil, 'No image URL found in OpenRouter response') unless image_url
42: 
43:           build_image_from_url(image_url, model)
44:         end
45: 
46:         def build_image_from_url(image_url, model)
47:           if image_url.start_with?('data:')
48:             # Parse data URL format: data:image/png;base64,<data>
49:             match = image_url.match(/^data:([^;]+);base64,(.+)$/)
50:             raise Error.new(nil, 'Invalid data URL format from OpenRouter') unless match
51: 
52:             Image.new(
53:               data: match[2],
54:               mime_type: match[1],
55:               model_id: model
56:             )
57:           else
58:             # Regular URL
59:             Image.new(
60:               url: image_url,
61:               mime_type: 'image/png',
62:               model_id: model
63:             )
64:           end
65:         end
66:       end
67:     end
68:   end
69: end

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class Mistral
6:       # Chat methods for Mistral API
7:       module Chat
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def format_role(role)
11:           role.to_s
12:         end
13: 
14:         def format_messages(messages)
15:           messages.map do |msg|
16:             {
17:               role: format_role(msg.role),
18:               content: format_content_with_thinking(msg),
19:               tool_calls: Protocols::ChatCompletions::Tools.format_tool_calls(msg.tool_calls),
20:               tool_call_id: msg.tool_call_id
21:             }.compact
22:           end
23:         end
24: 
25:         # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
26:         def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false,
27:                            schema: nil, thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
28:           payload = super
29:           payload.delete(:stream_options)
30:           configure_thinking_payload(payload, model, thinking)
31:           normalize_required_tool_choice(payload)
32:           payload
33:         end
34:         # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
35: 
36:         def build_tool_choice(tool_choice)
37:           return 'any' if tool_choice == :required
38: 
39:           Protocols::ChatCompletions::Tools.build_tool_choice(tool_choice)
40:         end
41: 
42:         def normalize_required_tool_choice(payload)
43:           return unless payload[:tool_choice] == 'any' && Array(payload[:tools]).one?
44: 
45:           function_name = payload.dig(:tools, 0, :function, :name)
46:           return unless function_name
47: 
48:           payload[:tool_choice] = {
49:             type: 'function',
50:             function: { name: function_name }
51:           }
52:         end
53: 
54:         def format_content_with_thinking(msg)
55:           formatted_content = Mistral::Media.format_content(msg.content)
56:           return formatted_content unless msg.role == :assistant && msg.thinking
57: 
58:           content_blocks = build_thinking_blocks(msg.thinking)
59:           append_formatted_content(content_blocks, formatted_content)
60: 
61:           content_blocks
62:         end
63: 
64:         def warn_on_unsupported_thinking(model, thinking)
65:           return unless thinking&.enabled?
66:           return if native_reasoning_model?(model.id) || adjustable_reasoning_model?(model.id)
67: 
68:           RubyLLM.logger.warn(
69:             'Mistral thinking is only supported on Magistral and adjustable-reasoning models. ' \
70:             "Ignoring thinking settings for #{model.id}."
71:           )
72:         end
73: 
74:         def configure_thinking_payload(payload, model, thinking)
75:           return unless thinking&.enabled?
76: 
77:           if native_reasoning_model?(model.id)
78:             configure_native_reasoning_payload(payload, thinking)
79:           elsif adjustable_reasoning_model?(model.id)
80:             payload[:reasoning_effort] = reasoning_effort_for(thinking)
81:           else
82:             payload.delete(:reasoning_effort)
83:             warn_on_unsupported_thinking(model, thinking)
84:           end
85:         end
86: 
87:         def configure_native_reasoning_payload(payload, thinking)
88:           payload.delete(:reasoning_effort)
89:           payload[:prompt_mode] = thinking.effort == 'none' ? nil : 'reasoning'
90:         end
91: 
92:         def reasoning_effort_for(thinking)
93:           effort = thinking.respond_to?(:effort) ? thinking.effort : nil
94:           return effort if %w[high none].include?(effort)
95: 
96:           'high'
97:         end
98: 
99:         def native_reasoning_model?(model_id)
100:           model_id.to_s.include?('magistral')
101:         end
102: 
103:         def adjustable_reasoning_model?(model_id)
104:           model_id.to_s.match?(/\Amistral-(?:small-latest|medium-(?:3(?:[.-]5)?|latest))\z/)
105:         end
106: 
107:         def build_thinking_blocks(thinking)
108:           return [] unless thinking
109: 
110:           if thinking.text
111:             [{
112:               type: 'thinking',
113:               thinking: [{ type: 'text', text: thinking.text }],
114:               signature: thinking.signature
115:             }.compact]
116:           elsif thinking.signature
117:             [{ type: 'thinking', signature: thinking.signature }]
118:           else
119:             []
120:           end
121:         end
122: 
123:         def append_formatted_content(content_blocks, formatted_content)
124:           if formatted_content.is_a?(Array)
125:             content_blocks.concat(formatted_content)
126:           elsif formatted_content
127:             content_blocks << { type: 'text', text: formatted_content }
128:           end
129:         end
130:       end
131:     end
132:   end
133: end

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class Azure < Provider
6:       # Azure's dialect of the Chat Completions API. Endpoints depend on how
7:       # the configured api_base is shaped (deployment, resource, or openai/v1).
8:       class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions
9:         AZURE_DEFAULT_CHAT_API_VERSION = '2024-05-01-preview'
10:         AZURE_DEFAULT_MODELS_API_VERSION = 'preview'
11: 
12:         include Azure::Chat
13:         include Azure::Embeddings
14:         include Azure::Media
15:         include Azure::Models
16: 
17:         def azure_endpoint(kind)
18:           parts = azure_base_parts
19: 
20:           case kind
21:           when :chat
22:             chat_endpoint(parts)
23:           when :embeddings
24:             embeddings_endpoint(parts)
25:           when :models
26:             models_endpoint(parts)
27:           else
28:             raise ArgumentError, "Unknown Azure endpoint kind: #{kind.inspect}"
29:           end
30:         end
31: 
32:         private
33: 
34:         def azure_base_parts
35:           @azure_base_parts ||= begin
36:             raw_base = @provider.api_base.to_s.sub(%r{/+\z}, '')
37:             version = raw_base[/[?&]api-version=([^&]+)/i, 1]
38:             path_base = raw_base.sub(/\?.*\z/, '')
39: 
40:             mode = if path_base.include?('/chat/completions')
41:                      :chat_endpoint
42:                    elsif path_base.include?('/openai/deployments/')
43:                      :deployment_base
44:                    elsif path_base.include?('/openai/v1')
45:                      :openai_v1_base
46:                    else
47:                      :resource_base
48:                    end
49: 
50:             {
51:               raw_base: raw_base,
52:               path_base: path_base,
53:               root: azure_host_root(path_base),
54:               mode: mode,
55:               version: version
56:             }
57:           end
58:         end
59: 
60:         def chat_endpoint(parts)
61:           case parts[:mode]
62:           when :chat_endpoint
63:             ''
64:           when :deployment_base
65:             with_api_version('chat/completions', parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_CHAT_API_VERSION)
66:           when :openai_v1_base
67:             with_api_version('chat/completions', parts[:version])
68:           else
69:             with_api_version('models/chat/completions', parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_CHAT_API_VERSION)
70:           end
71:         end
72: 
73:         def embeddings_endpoint(parts)
74:           case parts[:mode]
75:           when :deployment_base, :openai_v1_base
76:             with_api_version('embeddings', parts[:version])
77:           else
78:             "#{parts[:root]}/openai/v1/embeddings"
79:           end
80:         end
81: 
82:         def models_endpoint(parts)
83:           case parts[:mode]
84:           when :openai_v1_base
85:             with_api_version('models', parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_MODELS_API_VERSION)
86:           else
87:             "#{parts[:root]}/openai/v1/models?api-version=#{parts[:version] || AZURE_DEFAULT_MODELS_API_VERSION}"
88:           end
89:         end
90: 
91:         def with_api_version(path, version)
92:           return path unless version
93: 
94:           separator = path.include?('?') ? '&' : '?'
95:           "#{path}#{separator}api-version=#{version}"
96:         end
97: 
98:         def azure_host_root(base_without_query)
99:           base_without_query.sub(%r{/(models|openai)/.*\z}, '').sub(%r{/+\z}, '')
100:         end
101:       end
102:     end
103:   end
104: end

(End of file - total 104 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class Perplexity
6:       # Handles Perplexity Sonar media content.
7:       module Media
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         SUPPORTED_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS = %w[pdf doc docx txt rtf].freeze
11: 
12:         def format_content(content)
13:           Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_parts(content) do |attachment|
14:             case attachment.type
15:             when :image
16:               Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_image(attachment)
17:             when :pdf, :document
18:               format_document(attachment)
19:             when :text
20:               Protocols::ChatCompletions::Media.format_text_file(attachment)
21:             else
22:               raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
23:             end
24:           end
25:         end
26: 
27:         def format_document(attachment)
28:           raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless supported_file?(attachment)
29: 
30:           {
31:             type: 'file_url',
32:             file_url: {
33:               url: attachment.url? ? attachment.source.to_s : attachment.encoded
34:             }
35:           }
36:         end
37: 
38:         def supported_file?(attachment)
39:           return true if attachment.pdf?
40: 
41:           SUPPORTED_DOCUMENT_EXTENSIONS.include?(attachment.extension)
42:         end
43:       end
44:     end
45:   end
46: end

72:           gpt4o_mini: { input: 0.15, output: 0.6 },
73:           gpt4o_mini_audio: { input: 0.15, output: 0.6 },
74:           gpt4o_mini_realtime: { input: 0.6, output: 2.4 },
75:           gpt4o_mini_transcribe: { input: 1.25, output: 5.0 },
76:           gpt4o_mini_tts: { input: 0.6, output: 12.0 },
77:           gpt4o_realtime: { input: 5.0, output: 20.0 },
78:           gpt4o_search: { input: 2.5, output: 10.0 },
79:           gpt4o_transcribe: { input: 2.5, output: 10.0 },
80:           o1: { input: 15.0, output: 60.0 },
81:           o1_mini: { input: 1.1, output: 4.4 },
82:           o1_pro: { input: 150.0, output: 600.0 },
83:           o3_mini: { input: 1.1, output: 4.4 },
84:           babbage: { input: 0.4, output: 0.4 },
85:           davinci: { input: 2.0, output: 2.0 },
86:           embedding3_large: { price: 0.13 },
87:           embedding3_small: { price: 0.02 },
88:           embedding_ada: { price: 0.10 },
89:           tts1: { price: 15.0 },
90:           tts1_hd: { price: 30.0 },
91:           whisper: { price: 0.006 },
92:           moderation: { price: 0.0 }
93:         }.freeze
94: 
95:         NIL_LIMIT_FAMILIES = %w[
96:           gpt_image
97:           gpt_image_mini
98:           gpt_image15
99:           gpt4o_mini_tts
100:           tts1
101:           tts1_hd
102:           whisper
103:           moderation
104:           embedding3_large
105:           embedding3_small
106:           embedding_ada
107:         ].freeze
108: 
109:         def supports_tool_choice?(_model_id)
110:           true
111:         end
112: 
113:         def supports_tool_parallel_control?(_model_id)
114:           true
115:         end
116: 
117:         def context_window_for(model_id)
118:           family = model_family(model_id)
119:           return nil if NIL_LIMIT_FAMILIES.include?(family)
120: 
121:           case family
122:           when 'gpt41', 'gpt41_mini', 'gpt41_nano' then 1_047_576
123:           when 'gpt5', 'gpt5_mini', 'gpt5_nano', 'gpt4_turbo', 'gpt4o', 'gpt4o_audio', 'gpt4o_mini',
124:                'gpt4o_mini_audio', 'gpt4o_mini_realtime', 'gpt4o_realtime', 'gpt4o_search',
125:                'gpt4o_transcribe', 'o1_mini' then 128_000
126:           when 'gpt4' then 8_192
127:           when 'gpt4o_mini_transcribe' then 16_000
128:           when 'o1', 'o1_pro', 'o3_mini' then 200_000
129:           when 'gpt35_turbo' then 16_385
130:           else 4_096
131:           end
132:         end
133: 
134:         def max_tokens_for(model_id)
135:           family = model_family(model_id)
136:           return nil if NIL_LIMIT_FAMILIES.include?(family)
137: 
138:           case family
139:           when 'gpt5', 'gpt5_mini', 'gpt5_nano' then 400_000
140:           when 'gpt41', 'gpt41_mini', 'gpt41_nano' then 32_768
141:           when 'gpt4' then 8_192
142:           when 'gpt35_turbo' then 4_096
143:           when 'gpt4o_mini_transcribe' then 2_000
144:           when 'o1', 'o1_pro', 'o3_mini' then 100_000
145:           when 'o1_mini' then 65_536
146:           else 16_384
147:           end
148:         end
149: 
150:         def critical_capabilities_for(model_id)
151:           capabilities = []
152:           capabilities << 'function_calling' if supports_functions?(model_id)
153:           capabilities << 'structured_output' if supports_structured_output?(model_id)
154:           capabilities << 'vision' if supports_vision?(model_id)
155:           capabilities << 'reasoning' if model_id.match?(/o\d|gpt-5|codex/)
156:           # Web search models return url_citation annotations.
157:           capabilities << 'citations' if model_id.match?(/search-(?:preview|api)/)
158:           capabilities
159:         end
160: 
161:         def pricing_for(model_id)
162:           return image_pricing_for(model_id) if image_model?(model_id)
163: 
164:           standard_pricing = {
165:             input_per_million: input_price_for(model_id),
166:             output_per_million: output_price_for(model_id)
167:           }
168: 
169:           cached_price = cached_input_price_for(model_id)
170:           standard_pricing[:cache_read_input_per_million] = cached_price if cached_price
171: 
172:           { text_tokens: { standard: standard_pricing } }
173:         end
174: 
175:         def model_family(model_id)
176:           MODEL_PATTERNS.each do |family, pattern|
177:             return family.to_s if model_id.match?(pattern)
178:           end
179: 
180:           'other'
181:         end
182: 
183:         def supports_vision?(model_id)
184:           case model_family(model_id)
185:           when 'gpt_image', 'gpt_image_mini', 'gpt_image15', 'gpt5', 'gpt5_mini', 'gpt5_nano', 'gpt41', 'gpt41_mini',
186:                'gpt41_nano', 'gpt4', 'gpt4_turbo', 'gpt4o', 'gpt4o_mini', 'o1', 'o1_pro', 'moderation',
187:                'gpt4o_search'
188:             true
189:           else
190:             false
191:           end
192:         end
193: 
194:         def supports_functions?(model_id)
195:           case model_family(model_id)
196:           when 'gpt5', 'gpt5_mini', 'gpt5_nano', 'gpt41', 'gpt41_mini', 'gpt41_nano', 'gpt4',
197:                'gpt4_turbo', 'gpt4o', 'gpt4o_mini', 'o1', 'o1_pro', 'o3_mini'
198:             true
199:           else
200:             false
201:           end
202:         end
203: 
204:         def supports_structured_output?(model_id)
205:           case model_family(model_id)
206:           when 'gpt5', 'gpt5_mini', 'gpt5_nano', 'gpt41', 'gpt41_mini', 'gpt41_nano', 'gpt4o',
207:                'gpt4o_mini', 'o1', 'o1_pro', 'o3_mini'
208:             true
209:           else
210:             false
211:           end
212:         end
213: 
214:         def input_price_for(model_id)
215:           return family_prices(model_id).dig(:text, :input) if image_model?(model_id)
216: 
217:           price_for(model_id, :input, 0.50)
218:         end
219: 
220:         def output_price_for(model_id)
221:           return family_prices(model_id).dig(:text, :output) if image_model?(model_id)
222: 
223:           price_for(model_id, :output, 1.50)
224:         end
225: 
226:         def cached_input_price_for(model_id)
227:           return family_prices(model_id).dig(:text, :cached_input) if image_model?(model_id)
228: 
229:           family = model_family(model_id).to_sym
230:           PRICES.fetch(family, {})[:cached_input]
231:         end
232: 
233:         def image_model?(model_id)
234:           %w[gpt_image gpt_image_mini gpt_image15].include?(model_family(model_id))
235:         end
236: 
237:         def image_pricing_for(model_id)
238:           text_pricing = {
239:             input_per_million: input_price_for(model_id)
240:           }
241:           cached_text_price = cached_input_price_for(model_id)
242:           text_pricing[:cache_read_input_per_million] = cached_text_price if cached_text_price
243: 
244:           image_pricing = {
245:             input_per_million: family_prices(model_id).dig(:images, :input),
246:             output_per_million: family_prices(model_id).dig(:images, :output)
247:           }
248:           cached_image_price = family_prices(model_id).dig(:images, :cached_input)
249:           image_pricing[:cache_read_input_per_million] = cached_image_price if cached_image_price
250: 
251:           {
252:             text_tokens: { standard: text_pricing },
253:             images: { standard: image_pricing }
254:           }
255:         end
256: 
257:         def price_for(model_id, key, fallback)
258:           prices = family_prices(model_id)
259:           prices = { key => fallback } if prices.empty?
260:           prices[key] || prices[:price] || fallback
261:         end
262: 
263:         def family_prices(model_id)
264:           family = model_family(model_id).to_sym
265:           PRICES.fetch(family, {})
266:         end
267:       end
268:     end
269:   end
270: end

99:           case model_id
100:           when /gemini-2\.5-pro-exp-03-25/ then :pro_2_5 # rubocop:disable Naming/VariableNumber
101:           when /gemini-2\.0-flash-lite/ then :flash_lite_2 # rubocop:disable Naming/VariableNumber
102:           when /gemini-2\.0-flash/ then :flash_2 # rubocop:disable Naming/VariableNumber
103:           when /gemini-1\.5-flash-8b/ then :flash_8b
104:           when /gemini-1\.5-flash/ then :flash
105:           when /gemini-1\.5-pro/ then :pro

107:           when /text-embedding|embedding/ then :embedding
108:           when /imagen/ then :imagen
109:           when /aqa/ then :aqa
110:           else :base
111:           end

113:

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class Mistral
6:       # Determines capabilities for Mistral models
7:       module Capabilities
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def supports_streaming?(model_id)
11:           !model_id.match?(/embed|moderation|ocr|transcriptions/)
12:         end
13: 
14:         def supports_tools?(model_id)
15:           !model_id.match?(/embed|moderation|ocr|voxtral|transcriptions|mistral-(tiny|small)-(2312|2402)/)
16:         end
17: 
18:         def supports_tool_choice?(_model_id)
19:           true
20:         end
21: 
22:         def supports_tool_parallel_control?(_model_id)
23:           true
24:         end
25: 
26:         def supports_vision?(model_id)
27:           model_id.match?(/pixtral|mistral-small-(2503|2506)|mistral-medium/)
28:         end
29: 
30:         def supports_json_mode?(model_id)
31:           !model_id.match?(/embed|moderation|ocr|voxtral|transcriptions/) && supports_tools?(model_id)
32:         end
33: 
34:         def supports_reasoning?(model_id)
35:           model_id.match?(/magistral/) ||
36:             model_id.match?(/\Amistral-(?:small-latest|medium-(?:3(?:[.-]5)?|latest))\z/)
37:         end
38: 
39:         def format_display_name(model_id)
40:           case model_id
41:           when /mistral-large/ then 'Mistral Large'
42:           when /mistral-medium/ then 'Mistral Medium'
43:           when /mistral-small/ then 'Mistral Small'
44:           when /ministral-3b/ then 'Ministral 3B'
45:           when /ministral-8b/ then 'Ministral 8B'
46:           when /codestral/ then 'Codestral'
47:           when /pixtral-large/ then 'Pixtral Large'
48:           when /pixtral-12b/ then 'Pixtral 12B'
49:           when /mistral-embed/ then 'Mistral Embed'
50:           when /mistral-moderation/ then 'Mistral Moderation'
51:           else model_id.split('-').map(&:capitalize).join(' ')
52:           end
53:         end
54: 
55:         def model_family(model_id)
56:           case model_id
57:           when /mistral-large/ then 'mistral-large'
58:           when /mistral-medium/ then 'mistral-medium'
59:           when /mistral-small/ then 'mistral-small'
60:           when /ministral/ then 'ministral'
61:           when /codestral/ then 'codestral'
62:           when /pixtral/ then 'pixtral'
63:           when /mistral-embed/ then 'mistral-embed'
64:           when /mistral-moderation/ then 'mistral-moderation'
65:           else 'mistral'
66:           end
67:         end
68: 
69:         def context_window_for(_model_id)
70:           32_768
71:         end
72: 
73:         def max_tokens_for(_model_id)
74:           8192
75:         end
76: 
77:         def modalities_for(model_id)
78:           case model_id
79:           when /pixtral/
80:             {
81:               input: %w[text image],
82:               output: ['text']
83:             }
84:           when /embed/
85:             {
86:               input: ['text'],
87:               output: ['embeddings']
88:             }
89:           else
90:             {
91:               input: ['text'],
92:               output: ['text']
93:             }
94:           end
95:         end
96: 
97:         def capabilities_for(model_id) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
98:           case model_id
99:           when /moderation/ then ['moderation']
100:           when /voxtral.*transcribe/ then ['transcription']
101:           when /ocr/ then ['vision']
102:           else
103:             capabilities = []
104:             capabilities << 'streaming' if supports_streaming?(model_id)
105:             capabilities << 'function_calling' if supports_tools?(model_id)
106:             capabilities << 'structured_output' if supports_json_mode?(model_id)
107:             capabilities << 'vision' if supports_vision?(model_id)
108: 
109:             capabilities << 'reasoning' if supports_reasoning?(model_id)
110:             capabilities << 'batch' unless model_id.match?(/voxtral|ocr|embed|moderation/)
111:             capabilities << 'fine_tuning' if model_id.match?(/mistral-(small|medium|large)|devstral/)
112:             capabilities << 'distillation' if model_id.match?(/ministral/)
113:             capabilities << 'predicted_outputs' if model_id.match?(/codestral/)
114: 
115:             capabilities.uniq
116:           end
117:         end
118: 
119:         def pricing_for(_model_id)
120:           {
121:             input: 0.0,
122:             output: 0.0
123:           }
124:         end
125: 
126:         def release_date_for(model_id) # rubocop:disable Metrics/CyclomaticComplexity
127:           case model_id
128:           when 'open-mistral-7b', 'mistral-tiny' then '2023-09-27'
129:           when 'mistral-medium-2312', 'mistral-small-2312', 'mistral-small',
130:                'open-mixtral-8x7b', 'mistral-tiny-2312' then '2023-12-11'
131: 
132:           when 'mistral-embed' then '2024-01-11'
133:           when 'mistral-large-2402', 'mistral-small-2402' then '2024-02-26'
134:           when 'open-mixtral-8x22b', 'open-mixtral-8x22b-2404' then '2024-04-17'
135:           when 'codestral-2405' then '2024-05-22'
136:           when 'codestral-mamba-2407', 'codestral-mamba-latest', 'open-codestral-mamba' then '2024-07-16'
137:           when 'open-mistral-nemo', 'open-mistral-nemo-2407', 'mistral-tiny-2407',
138:                'mistral-tiny-latest' then '2024-07-18'
139:           when 'mistral-large-2407' then '2024-07-24'
140:           when 'pixtral-12b-2409', 'pixtral-12b-latest', 'pixtral-12b' then '2024-09-17'
141:           when 'mistral-small-2409' then '2024-09-18'
142:           when 'ministral-3b-2410', 'ministral-3b-latest', 'ministral-8b-2410',
143:                'ministral-8b-latest' then '2024-10-16'
144:           when 'pixtral-large-2411', 'pixtral-large-latest', 'mistral-large-pixtral-2411' then '2024-11-12'
145:           when 'mistral-large-2411', 'mistral-large-latest', 'mistral-large' then '2024-11-20'
146:           when 'codestral-2411-rc5', 'mistral-moderation-2411', 'mistral-moderation-latest' then '2024-11-26'
147:           when 'codestral-2412' then '2024-12-17'
148: 
149:           when 'mistral-small-2501' then '2025-01-13'
150:           when 'codestral-2501' then '2025-01-14'
151:           when 'mistral-saba-2502', 'mistral-saba-latest' then '2025-02-18'
152:           when 'mistral-small-2503' then '2025-03-03'
153:           when 'mistral-ocr-2503' then '2025-03-21'
154:           when 'mistral-medium', 'mistral-medium-latest', 'mistral-medium-2505' then '2025-05-06'
155:           when 'codestral-embed', 'codestral-embed-2505' then '2025-05-21'
156:           when 'mistral-ocr-2505', 'mistral-ocr-latest' then '2025-05-23'
157:           when 'devstral-small-2505' then '2025-05-28'
158:           when 'mistral-small-2506', 'mistral-small-latest', 'magistral-medium-2506',
159:                'magistral-medium-latest' then '2025-06-10'
160:           when 'devstral-small-2507', 'devstral-small-latest', 'devstral-medium-2507',

7:       module Capabilities
8:         module_function
9:

11:         DEFAULT_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS = 384_000
12:         DEFAULT_PRICES = {
13:           input: 0.14,
14:           output: 0.28,
15:           cache_read: 0.0028

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/ruby_llm/lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity/capabilities.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class Perplexity
6:       # Provider-level capability checks and narrow registry fallbacks.
7:       module Capabilities
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         PRICES = {
11:           sonar: { input: 1.0, output: 1.0 },
12:           sonar_pro: { input: 3.0, output: 15.0 },
13:           sonar_reasoning: { input: 1.0, output: 5.0 },
14:           sonar_reasoning_pro: { input: 2.0, output: 8.0 },
15:           sonar_deep_research: {
16:             input: 2.0,
17:             output: 8.0,
18:             reasoning_output: 3.0
19:           }
20:         }.freeze
21: 
22:         def supports_tool_choice?(_model_id)
23:           false
24:         end
25: 
26:         def supports_tool_parallel_control?(_model_id)
27:           false
28:         end
29: 
30:         def context_window_for(model_id)
31:           model_id.match?(/sonar-pro/) ? 200_000 : 128_000
32:         end
33: 
34:         def max_tokens_for(model_id)
35:           model_id.match?(/sonar-(?:pro|reasoning-pro)/) ? 8_192 : 4_096
36:         end
37: 
38:         def critical_capabilities_for(model_id)
39:           # Every Perplexity model returns search result citations.
40:           capabilities = ['citations']
41:           capabilities << 'vision' if model_id.match?(/sonar(?:-pro|-reasoning(?:-pro)?)?$/)
42:           capabilities << 'reasoning' if model_id.match?(/reasoning|deep-research/)
43:           capabilities
44:         end
45: 
46:         def pricing_for(model_id)
47:           prices = PRICES.fetch(model_family(model_id), { input: 1.0, output: 1.0 })
48: 
49:           standard = {
50:             input_per_million: prices[:input],
51:             output_per_million: prices[:output]
52:           }
53:           standard[:reasoning_output_per_million] = prices[:reasoning_output] if prices[:reasoning_output]
54: 
55:           { text_tokens: { standard: standard } }
56:         end
57: 
58:         def model_family(model_id)
59:           case model_id
60:           when 'sonar' then :sonar
61:           when 'sonar-pro' then :sonar_pro
62:           when 'sonar-reasoning' then :sonar_reasoning
63:           when 'sonar-reasoning-pro' then :sonar_reasoning_pro
64:           when 'sonar-deep-research' then :sonar_deep_research
65:           else :unknown
66:           end
67:         end
68: 
69:         module_function :context_window_for, :max_tokens_for, :critical_capabilities_for, :pricing_for, :model_family
70:       end
71:     end
72:   end
73: end

(End of file - total 73 lines)
</content>
