1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'spec_helper'
4: 
5: RSpec.describe RubyLLM::Provider do
6:   def api_base_cases
7:     {
8:       anthropic: {
9:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Anthropic,
10:         key: :anthropic_api_base,
11:         custom: 'https://anthropic-proxy.example.com',
12:         default: 'https://api.anthropic.com'
13:       },
14:       azure: {
15:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Azure,
16:         key: :azure_api_base,
17:         custom: 'https://azure-resource.example.com'
18:       },
19:       bedrock: {
20:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Bedrock,
21:         key: :bedrock_api_base,
22:         custom: 'https://bedrock-proxy.example.com',
23:         default: 'https://bedrock-runtime.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'
24:       },
25:       deepseek: {
26:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::DeepSeek,
27:         key: :deepseek_api_base,
28:         custom: 'https://deepseek-proxy.example.com',
29:         default: 'https://api.deepseek.com'
30:       },
31:       gemini: {
32:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Gemini,
33:         key: :gemini_api_base,
34:         custom: 'https://gemini-proxy.example.com/v1',
35:         default: 'https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta'
36:       },
37:       gpustack: {
38:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::GPUStack,
39:         key: :gpustack_api_base,
40:         custom: 'https://gpustack.example.com/v1'
41:       },
42:       mistral: {
43:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Mistral,
44:         key: :mistral_api_base,
45:         custom: 'https://mistral-proxy.example.com/v1',
46:         default: 'https://api.mistral.ai/v1'
47:       },
48:       ollama: {
49:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Ollama,
50:         key: :ollama_api_base,
51:         custom: 'https://ollama.example.com/v1'
52:       },
53:       openai: {
54:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI,
55:         key: :openai_api_base,
56:         custom: 'https://openai-proxy.example.com/v1',
57:         default: 'https://api.openai.com/v1'
58:       },
59:       openrouter: {
60:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::OpenRouter,
61:         key: :openrouter_api_base,
62:         custom: 'https://openrouter-proxy.example.com/api/v1',
63:         default: 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'
64:       },
65:       perplexity: {
66:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::Perplexity,
67:         key: :perplexity_api_base,
68:         custom: 'https://perplexity-proxy.example.com',
69:         default: 'https://api.perplexity.ai'
70:       },
71:       vertexai: {
72:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::VertexAI,
73:         key: :vertexai_api_base,
74:         custom: 'https://vertex-proxy.example.com/v1beta1',
75:         default: 'https://us-east1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1'
76:       },
77:       xai: {
78:         provider: RubyLLM::Providers::XAI,
79:         key: :xai_api_base,
80:         custom: 'https://xai-proxy.example.com/v1',
81:         default: 'https://api.x.ai/v1'
82:       }
83:     }
84:   end
85: 
86:   def config_for(slug)
87:     RubyLLM::Configuration.new.tap do |config|
88:       case slug
89:       when :anthropic
90:         config.anthropic_api_key = 'anthropic-key'
91:       when :azure
92:         config.azure_api_key = 'azure-key'
93:       when :bedrock
94:         config.bedrock_api_key = 'bedrock-key'
95:         config.bedrock_secret_key = 'bedrock-secret'
96:         config.bedrock_region = 'us-east-1'
97:       when :deepseek
98:         config.deepseek_api_key = 'deepseek-key'
99:       when :gemini
100:         config.gemini_api_key = 'gemini-key'
101:       when :gpustack
102:         config.gpustack_api_key = 'gpustack-key'
103:       when :mistral
104:         config.mistral_api_key = 'mistral-key'
105:       when :ollama
106:         config.ollama_api_key = 'ollama-key'
107:       when :openai
108:         config.openai_api_key = 'openai-key'
109:       when :openrouter
110:         config.openrouter_api_key = 'openrouter-key'
111:       when :perplexity
112:         config.perplexity_api_key = 'perplexity-key'
113:       when :vertexai
114:         config.vertexai_project_id = 'vertex-project'
115:         config.vertexai_location = 'us-east1'
116:       when :xai
117:         config.xai_api_key = 'xai-key'
118:       end
119:     end
120:   end
121: 
122:   describe '.register' do
123:     it 'registers provider configuration options on Configuration' do
124:       provider_key = :test_provider_spec
125:       option_keys = %i[test_provider_api_key test_provider_api_base]
126: 
127:       provider_class = Class.new(described_class) do
128:         class << self
129:           def configuration_options
130:             %i[test_provider_api_key test_provider_api_base]
131:           end
132: 
133:           def configuration_requirements
134:             %i[test_provider_api_key]
135:           end
136:         end
137:       end
138: 
139:       original_providers = described_class.providers.dup
140: 
141:       begin
142:         described_class.register(provider_key, provider_class)
143: 
144:         config = RubyLLM::Configuration.new
145:         option_keys.each do |key|
146:           expect(config).to respond_to(key)
147:           expect(config).to respond_to("#{key}=")
148:         end
149:       ensure
150:         described_class.providers.replace(original_providers)
151:         option_keys.each do |key|
152:           RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:option_keys).delete(key)
153:           RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:defaults).delete(key)
154:           RubyLLM::Configuration.class_eval do
155:             remove_method key if method_defined?(key)
156:             remove_method :"#{key}=" if method_defined?(:"#{key}=")
157:           end
158:         end
159:       end
160:     end
161:   end
162: 
163:   describe '.configured?' do
164:     it 'treats blank required configuration as missing' do
165:       provider_class = Class.new(described_class) do
166:         class << self
167:           def configuration_options
168:             %i[blank_test_api_key]
169:           end
170: 
171:           def configuration_requirements
172:             %i[blank_test_api_key]
173:           end
174:         end
175:       end
176: 
177:       RubyLLM::Configuration.register_provider_options(provider_class.configuration_options)
178:       config = RubyLLM::Configuration.new
179:       config.blank_test_api_key = ''
180: 
181:       expect(provider_class.configured?(config)).to be(false)
182:     ensure
183:       RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:option_keys).delete(:blank_test_api_key)
184:       RubyLLM::Configuration.send(:defaults).delete(:blank_test_api_key)
185:       RubyLLM::Configuration.class_eval do
186:         remove_method :blank_test_api_key if method_defined?(:blank_test_api_key)
187:         remove_method :blank_test_api_key= if method_defined?(:blank_test_api_key=)
188:       end
189:     end
190:   end
191: 
192:   describe 'provider configuration schema' do
193:     it 'keeps requirements as a subset of declared configuration options' do
194:       described_class.providers.each_value do |provider_class|
195:         missing = provider_class.configuration_requirements - provider_class.configuration_options
196:         expect(missing).to be_empty, "#{provider_class.name} is missing options for requirements: #{missing.inspect}"
197:       end
198:     end
199: 
200:     it 'exposes aggregated provider options through Configuration' do
201:       expect(RubyLLM::Configuration.options).to include(
202:         :openrouter_api_base,
203:         :deepseek_api_base,
204:         :ollama_api_key
205:       )
206: 
207:       expect(RubyLLM::Configuration.options).to include(
208:         :request_timeout,
209:         :model_registry_class
210:       )
211:     end
212:   end
213: 
214:   context 'with API base configuration' do
215:     it 'covers every registered provider' do
216:       expect(api_base_cases.keys).to match_array(described_class.providers.keys)
217:     end
218: 
219:     it 'registers an API base option for every provider' do
220:       expected_options = api_base_cases.values.map { |data| data[:key] }
221: 
222:       expect(RubyLLM::Configuration.options).to include(*expected_options)
223:     end
224: 
225:     it 'uses the configured API base for every provider' do
226:       api_base_cases.each do |slug, data|
227:         config = config_for(slug)
228:         config.public_send("#{data[:key]}=", data[:custom])
229: 
230:         expect(data[:provider].new(config).api_base).to eq(data[:custom])
231:       end
232:     end
233: 
234:     it 'keeps existing defaults for providers with built-in endpoints' do
235:       api_base_cases.each do |slug, data|
236:         next unless data[:default]
237: 
238:         expect(data[:provider].new(config_for(slug)).api_base).to eq(data[:default])
239:       end
240:     end
241:   end
242: 
243:   describe 'protocol resolution' do
244:     let(:provider) { RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.new(config_for(:openai)) }
245:     let(:model) { instance_double(RubyLLM::Model::Info, id: 'gpt-5.4') }
246: 
247:     it 'defaults to the first declared protocol' do
248:       expect(RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.default_protocol).to eq(:responses)
249:       expect(RubyLLM::Providers::Mistral.default_protocol).to eq(:chat_completions)
250:     end
251: 
252:     it 'routes through protocol_for by default' do
253:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, nil, model)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::Responses)
254:     end
255: 
256:     it 'routes chat-completions-only models away from the default' do
257:       audio = instance_double(RubyLLM::Model::Info, id: 'gpt-audio-mini')
258: 
259:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, nil, audio)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions)
260:     end
261: 
262:     it 'prefers the configured protocol over routing' do
263:       config = config_for(:openai)
264:       config.openai_protocol = :chat_completions
265: 
266:       provider = RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.new(config)
267: 
268:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, nil, model)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions)
269:     end
270: 
271:     it 'prefers an explicit protocol over the configured one' do
272:       config = config_for(:openai)
273:       config.openai_protocol = :chat_completions
274: 
275:       provider = RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI.new(config)
276: 
277:       expect(provider.send(:resolve_protocol, :responses, model)).to eq(RubyLLM::Protocols::Responses)
278:     end

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/ruby_llm/lib/ruby_llm/streaming.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'event_stream_parser'
4: require 'faraday'
5: require 'json'
6: 
7: module RubyLLM
8:   # Handles streaming responses from AI providers.
9:   module Streaming
10:     module_function
11: 
12:     def stream_response(payload, additional_headers = {}, &block)
13:       accumulator = StreamAccumulator.new
14:       on_data = handle_stream do |chunk|
15:         accumulator.add chunk
16:         block.call chunk
17:       end
18: 
19:       response = @connection.post stream_url, payload do |req|
20:         req.headers = additional_headers.merge(req.headers) unless additional_headers.empty?
21:         if faraday_1?
22:           req.options[:on_data] = on_data
23:         else
24:           req.options.on_data = on_data
25:         end
26:       end
27: 
28:       message = accumulator.to_message(response)
29:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Stream completed: #{message.content}" }
30:       message
31:     end
32: 
33:     def handle_stream(&block)
34:       build_on_data_handler do |data|
35:         block.call(build_chunk(data)) if data.is_a?(Hash)
36:       end
37:     end
38: 
39:     private
40: 
41:     def faraday_1?
42:       Faraday::VERSION.start_with?('1')
43:     end
44: 
45:     def build_on_data_handler(&handler)
46:       buffer = +''
47:       parser = EventStreamParser::Parser.new
48: 
49:       FaradayHandlers.build(
50:         faraday_v1: faraday_1?,
51:         on_chunk: ->(chunk, env) { process_stream_chunk(chunk, parser, env, &handler) },
52:         on_failed_response: ->(chunk, env) { handle_failed_response(chunk, buffer, env) }
53:       )
54:     end
55: 
56:     def process_stream_chunk(chunk, parser, env, &)
57:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Received chunk: #{chunk}" } if RubyLLM.config.log_stream_debug
58: 
59:       if error_chunk?(chunk)
60:         handle_error_chunk(chunk, env)
61:       elsif json_error_payload?(chunk)
62:         handle_json_error_chunk(chunk, env)
63:       else
64:         handle_sse(chunk, parser, env, &)
65:       end
66:     end
67: 
68:     def error_chunk?(chunk)
69:       chunk.start_with?('event: error')
70:     end
71: 
72:     def json_error_payload?(chunk)
73:       chunk.lstrip.start_with?('{') && chunk.include?('"error"')
74:     end
75: 
76:     def handle_json_error_chunk(chunk, env)
77:       parse_error_from_json(chunk, env, 'Failed to parse JSON error chunk')
78:     end
79: 
80:     def handle_error_chunk(chunk, env)
81:       error_data = chunk.split("\n")[1].delete_prefix('data: ')
82:       parse_error_from_json(error_data, env, 'Failed to parse error chunk')
83:     end
84: 
85:     def handle_failed_response(chunk, buffer, env)
86:       buffer << chunk
87:       error_data = JSON.parse(buffer)
88:       raise_stream_error(buffer, error_data, env)
89:     rescue JSON::ParserError
90:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Accumulating error chunk: #{chunk}" }
91:     end
92: 
93:     def handle_sse(chunk, parser, env)
94:       parser.feed(chunk) do |type, data|
95:         case type.to_sym
96:         when :error
97:           handle_error_event(data, env)
98:         else
99:           yield handle_data(data, env) unless data == '[DONE]'
100:         end
101:       end
102:     end
103: 
104:     def handle_data(data, env)
105:       parsed = JSON.parse(data)
106:       return parsed unless parsed.is_a?(Hash) && parsed.key?('error')
107: 
108:       raise_stream_error(data, parsed, env)
109:     rescue JSON::ParserError => e
110:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Failed to parse data chunk: #{e.message}" }
111:     end
112: 
113:     def handle_error_event(data, env)
114:       parse_error_from_json(data, env, 'Failed to parse error event')
115:     end
116: 
117:     def parse_streaming_error(data)
118:       error_data = JSON.parse(data)
119:       [500, error_data['message'] || 'Unknown streaming error']
120:     rescue JSON::ParserError => e
121:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Failed to parse streaming error: #{e.message}" }
122:       [500, "Failed to parse error: #{data}"]
123:     end
124: 
125:     def raise_stream_error(raw_data, parsed_data, env)
126:       status, _message = parse_streaming_error(raw_data)
127:       error_response = build_stream_error_response(parsed_data, env, status)
128:       ErrorMiddleware.parse_error(provider: self, response: error_response)
129:     end
130: 
131:     def parse_error_from_json(data, env, error_message)
132:       parsed_data = JSON.parse(data)
133:       raise_stream_error(data, parsed_data, env)
134:     rescue JSON::ParserError => e
135:       RubyLLM.logger.debug { "#{error_message}: #{e.message}" }
136:     end
137: 
138:     def build_stream_error_response(parsed_data, env, status)
139:       error_status = status || env&.status || 500
140: 
141:       if faraday_1?
142:         Struct.new(:body, :status).new(parsed_data, error_status)
143:       else
144:         env.merge(body: parsed_data, status: error_status)
145:       end
146:     end
147: 
148:     # Builds Faraday on_data handlers for different major versions.
149:     module FaradayHandlers
150:       module_function
151: 
152:       def build(faraday_v1:, on_chunk:, on_failed_response:)
153:         if faraday_v1
154:           v1_on_data(on_chunk)
155:         else
156:           v2_on_data(on_chunk, on_failed_response)
157:         end
158:       end
159: 
160:       def v1_on_data(on_chunk)
161:         proc do |chunk, _size|
162:           on_chunk.call(chunk, nil)
163:         end
164:       end
165: 
166:       def v2_on_data(on_chunk, on_failed_response)
167:         proc do |chunk, _bytes, env|
168:           if env&.status == 200
169:             on_chunk.call(chunk, env)
170:           else
171:             on_failed_response.call(chunk, env)
172:           end
173:         end
174:       end
175:     end
176:   end
177: end

(End of file - total 177 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'spec_helper'
4: 
5: RSpec.describe RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions::Models do
6:   describe '.parse_list_models_response' do
7:     let(:response_class) { Struct.new(:body) }
8:     let(:capabilities) { RubyLLM::Providers::OpenAI::Capabilities }
9: 
10:     let(:response) do
11:       instance_double(
12:         response_class,
13:         body: {
14:           'data' => [
15:             {
16:               'id' => 'gpt-3.5-turbo-0125',
17:               'created' => 1_741_110_400,
18:               'object' => 'model',
19:               'owned_by' => 'system'
20:             },
21:             {
22:               'id' => 'omni-moderation-latest',
23:               'created' => 1_741_110_401,
24:               'object' => 'model',
25:               'owned_by' => 'system'
26:             }
27:           ]
28:         }
29:       )
30:     end
31: 
32:     def parse_models(response_body, capabilities)
33:       described_class.parse_list_models_response(
34:         instance_double(response_class, body: response_body),
35:         'openai',
36:         capabilities
37:       )
38:     end
39: 
40:     def parsed_model(id, capabilities)
41:       parse_models(response.body, capabilities).find { |entry| entry.id == id }
42:     end
43: 
44:     it 'restores only critical fallback metadata for sparse models' do
45:       model = parsed_model('gpt-3.5-turbo-0125', capabilities)
46: 
47:       expect(model.name).to eq('gpt-3.5-turbo-0125')
48:       expect(model.family).to be_nil
49:       expect(model.context_window).to eq(16_385)
50:       expect(model.max_output_tokens).to eq(4096)
51:       expect(model.capabilities).to eq([])
52:       expect(model.pricing.to_h).to eq(
53:         text_tokens: {
54:           standard: {
55:             input_per_million: 0.5,
56:             output_per_million: 1.5
57:           }
58:         }
59:       )
60:       expect(model.metadata).to eq(object: 'model', owned_by: 'system')
61:     end
62: 
63:     it 'restores critical capabilities for reasoning models' do
64:       reasoning_model = parse_models(
65:         {
66:           'data' => [
67:             {
68:               'id' => 'gpt-5.4-nano',
69:               'created' => 1_741_110_402,
70:               'object' => 'model',
71:               'owned_by' => 'system'
72:             }
73:           ]
74:         },
75:         capabilities
76:       ).first
77: 
78:       expect(reasoning_model.capabilities).to contain_exactly(
79:         'function_calling',
80:         'structured_output',
81:         'vision',
82:         'reasoning'
83:       )
84:     end
85: 
86:     it 'restores only critical capabilities to moderation models' do
87:       model = parsed_model('omni-moderation-latest', capabilities)
88: 
89:       expect(model.capabilities).to eq(['vision'])
90:       expect(model.pricing.to_h).to eq({})
91:     end
92:   end
93: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'spec_helper'
4: 
5: RSpec.describe RubyLLM::Chat do
6:   include_context 'with configured RubyLLM'
7: 
8:   def basic_chat(model:, provider:)
9:     chat = RubyLLM.chat(model: model, provider: provider)
10:     return chat.with_params(enable_thinking: false) if provider == :gpustack && model == 'qwen3'
11: 
12:     chat
13:   end
14: 
15:   def total_input_tokens(message)
16:     message.input_tokens.to_i + message.cached_tokens.to_i + message.cache_creation_tokens.to_i
17:   end
18: 
19:   def expect_token_usage(message)
20:     expect(total_input_tokens(message)).to be_positive
21:     expect(message.output_tokens).to be_positive
22:   end
23: 
24:   describe 'basic chat functionality' do
25:     CHAT_MODELS.each do |model_info|
26:       model = model_info[:model]
27:       provider = model_info[:provider]
28:       it "#{provider}/#{model} can have a basic conversation" do
29:         chat = basic_chat(model: model, provider: provider)
30:         response = chat.ask("What's 2 + 2?")
31: 
32:         expect(response.content).to include('4')
33:         expect(response.role).to eq(:assistant)
34:         expect_token_usage(response)
35:       end
36: 
37:       it "#{provider}/#{model} returns raw responses" do
38:         chat = RubyLLM.chat(model: model, provider: provider)
39:         response = chat.ask('What is the capital of France?')
40:         expect(response.raw).to be_present
41:         expect(response.raw.headers).to be_present
42:         expect(response.raw.body).to be_present
43:         expect(response.raw.status).to be_present
44:         expect(response.raw.status).to eq(200)
45:         expect(response.raw.env.request_body).to be_present
46:       end
47: 
48:       it "#{provider}/#{model} can handle multi-turn conversations" do
49:         chat = basic_chat(model: model, provider: provider)
50: 
51:         first = chat.ask('Who is the creator of the programming language Ruby?')
52:         expect(first.content).to include('Matz')
53: 
54:         followup = chat.ask('What year did he create Ruby?')
55:         expect(followup.content).to include('199')
56:       end
57: 
58:       it "#{provider}/#{model} successfully uses the system prompt" do
59:         chat = RubyLLM.chat(model: model, provider: provider).with_temperature(0.0)
60: 
61:         # Use a distinctive and unusual instruction that wouldn't happen naturally
62:         chat.with_instructions 'You must include the exact phrase "XKCD7392" somewhere in your response.'
63: 
64:         response = chat.ask('Tell me about the weather.')
65:         expect(response.content).to match(/XKCD7392/i)
66:       end
67: 
68:       it "#{provider}/#{model} replaces previous system messages when replace: true" do
69:         if %i[perplexity mistral].include?(provider)
70:           skip 'Provider API does not allow system messages after user/assistant messages'
71:         end
72:         skip 'xAI may retain prior instruction artifacts from conversation history' if provider == :xai
73: 
74:         if provider == :ollama && model == 'qwen3'
75:           skip 'ollama/qwen3 includes thinking tags even with enable_thinking: false'
76:         end
77: 
78:         chat = RubyLLM.chat(model: model, provider: provider).with_temperature(0.0)
79:         chat = chat.with_params(enable_thinking: false) if provider == :gpustack && model == 'qwen3'

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'spec_helper'
4: 
5: RSpec.describe RubyLLM::ErrorMiddleware do
6:   describe '.parse_error' do
7:     let(:provider) { instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: 'provider error') }
8: 
9:     it 'maps 502 to ServiceUnavailableError' do
10:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(502, '{"error":{"message":"down"}}')
11: 
12:       expect do
13:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
14:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ServiceUnavailableError)
15:     end
16: 
17:     it 'maps 503 to ServiceUnavailableError' do
18:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(503, '{"error":{"message":"down"}}')
19: 
20:       expect do
21:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
22:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ServiceUnavailableError)
23:     end
24: 
25:     it 'maps 504 to ServiceUnavailableError' do
26:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(504, '{"error":{"message":"timeout"}}')
27: 
28:       expect do
29:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
30:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ServiceUnavailableError)
31:     end
32: 
33:     it 'maps context-length-like 429 errors to ContextLengthExceededError' do
34:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(429, '{"error":{"message":"Request too large for model"}}')
35:       provider = instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: 'Request too large for model')
36: 
37:       expect do
38:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
39:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ContextLengthExceededError)
40:     end
41: 
42:     it 'keeps regular 429 errors as RateLimitError' do
43:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(429, '{"error":{"message":"Rate limit exceeded"}}')
44:       provider = instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: 'Rate limit exceeded')
45: 
46:       expect do
47:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
48:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::RateLimitError)
49:     end
50: 
51:     it 'maps context-length-like 400 errors to ContextLengthExceededError' do
52:       msg = "This model's maximum context length is 8192 tokens."
53:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(400, %({"error":{"message":"#{msg}"}}))
54:       provider = instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: msg)
55: 
56:       expect do
57:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
58:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ContextLengthExceededError)
59:     end
60: 
61:     it "maps Anthropic's 'prompt is too long' 400 error to ContextLengthExceededError" do
62:       msg = 'prompt is too long: 209025 tokens > 200000 maximum'
63:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(400, %({"error":{"message":"#{msg}"}}))
64:       provider = instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: msg)
65: 
66:       expect do
67:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
68:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::ContextLengthExceededError)
69:     end
70: 
71:     it 'keeps regular 400 errors as BadRequestError' do
72:       response = Struct.new(:status, :body).new(400, '{"error":{"message":"Invalid model specified"}}')
73:       provider = instance_double(RubyLLM::Provider, parse_error: 'Invalid model specified')
74: 
75:       expect do
76:         described_class.parse_error(provider: provider, response: response)
77:       end.to raise_error(RubyLLM::BadRequestError)
78:     end
79:   end
80: end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'rails/generators'
4: require 'rails/generators/active_record'
5: require_relative '../generator_helpers'
6: 
7: module RubyLLM
8:   module Generators
9:     # Generator for RubyLLM Rails models and migrations
10:     class InstallGenerator < Rails::Generators::Base
11:       include Rails::Generators::Migration
12:       include RubyLLM::Generators::GeneratorHelpers
13: 
14:       namespace 'ruby_llm:install'
15: 
16:       source_root File.expand_path('templates', __dir__)
17: 
18:       argument :model_mappings, type: :array, default: [], banner: 'chat:ChatName message:MessageName ...'
19: 
20:       class_option :skip_active_storage, type: :boolean, default: false,
21:                                          desc: 'Skip ActiveStorage installation and attachment setup'
22: 
23:       desc 'Creates models and migrations for RubyLLM Rails integration\n' \
24:            'Usage: bin/rails g ruby_llm:install [chat:ChatName] [message:MessageName] ...'
25: 
26:       def self.next_migration_number(dirname)
27:         ::ActiveRecord::Generators::Base.next_migration_number(dirname)
28:       end
29: 
30:       def create_migration_files
31:         migration_template 'create_chats_migration.rb.tt',
32:                            "db/migrate/create_#{chat_table_name}.rb"
33:         migration_template 'create_messages_migration.rb.tt',
34:                            "db/migrate/create_#{message_table_name}.rb"
35:         migration_template 'create_tool_calls_migration.rb.tt',
36:                            "db/migrate/create_#{tool_call_table_name}.rb"
37:         migration_template 'create_models_migration.rb.tt',
38:                            "db/migrate/create_#{model_table_name}.rb"
39:         migration_template 'add_references_to_chats_tool_calls_and_messages_migration.rb.tt',
40:                            'db/migrate/add_references_to_' \
41:                            "#{chat_table_name}_#{tool_call_table_name}_and_#{message_table_name}.rb"
42:       end
43: 
44:       def create_model_files
45:         create_namespace_modules
46: 
47:         template 'chat_model.rb.tt', "app/models/#{chat_model_name.underscore}.rb"
48:         template 'message_model.rb.tt', "app/models/#{message_model_name.underscore}.rb"
49:         template 'tool_call_model.rb.tt', "app/models/#{tool_call_model_name.underscore}.rb"
50: 
51:         template 'model_model.rb.tt', "app/models/#{model_model_name.underscore}.rb"
52:       end
53: 
54:       def create_initializer
55:         template 'initializer.rb.tt', 'config/initializers/ruby_llm.rb'
56:       end
57: 
58:       def create_convention_directories
59:         %w[agents tools schemas prompts].each do |name|
60:           empty_directory "app/#{name}"
61:           create_file "app/#{name}/.gitkeep" unless File.exist?(Rails.root.join("app/#{name}/.gitkeep"))
62:         end
63:       end
64: 
65:       def install_active_storage
66:         return if options[:skip_active_storage]
67: 
68:         say '  Installing ActiveStorage for file attachments...', :cyan
69:         rails_command 'active_storage:install'
70:       end
71: 
72:       def show_install_info
73:         say "\n  ✅ RubyLLM installed!", :green
74: 
75:         say '  ✅ ActiveStorage configured for file attachments support', :green unless options[:skip_active_storage]
76: 
77:         say "\n  Next steps:", :yellow
78:         say '     1. Run: bin/rails db:migrate'
79:         say '     2. Run: bin/rails ruby_llm:load_models'
80:         say '     3. Set your API keys in config/initializers/ruby_llm.rb'
81: 
82:         say "     4. Start chatting: #{chat_model_name}.create!(model: 'gpt-5-nano').ask('Hello!')"
83:         say "     5. Optional UI: #{chat_ui_generator_command}"
84: 
85:         if options[:skip_active_storage]
86:           say "\n  📎 Note: ActiveStorage was skipped", :yellow
87:           say '     File attachments won\'t work without ActiveStorage.'
88:           say '     To enable later:'
89:           say '       1. Run: bin/rails active_storage:install && bin/rails db:migrate'
90:           say "       2. Add to your #{message_model_name} model: has_many_attached :attachments"
91:         end
92: 
93:         say "\n  📚 Documentation: https://rubyllm.com", :cyan
94: 
95:         say "\n  ❤️  Love RubyLLM?", :magenta
96:         say '     • ⭐ Star on GitHub: https://github.com/crmne/ruby_llm'
97:         say '     • 🐦 Follow for updates: https://x.com/paolino'
98:         say "\n"
99:       end
100: 
101:       private
102: 
103:       def chat_ui_generator_command
104:         mappings = model_mappings.join(' ')
105:         mappings = " #{mappings}" unless mappings.empty?
106:         "bin/rails generate ruby_llm:chat_ui#{mappings}"
107:       end
108:     end
109:   end
110: end

(End of file - total 110 lines)

Step 4: Find settings declaration and validation
Step 5: Trace HTTP failure to typed errors

Step 7: Produce complete edit map

content.text:
## Step 1: Orient in the codebase

**RubyLLM** is a unified Ruby client for LLM providers. Code is organized around a provider registry, a protocol layer per API dialect, and a model registry.

- Public entry / module: `lib/ruby_llm.rb`
- Provider base + registry: `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb`
- Protocol base: `lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb`
- Shared API dialect implementations: `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/`
  - `chat_completions/` — OpenAI-compatible APIs (the dialect a new integration will likely use)
  - `anthropic/`, `gemini/`, `converse/`, `responses/`
- Provider integrations: `lib/ruby_llm/providers/`
- Model registry + resolution: `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb` and `lib/ruby_llm/models.json`
- Configuration: `lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb`
- Errors: `lib/ruby_llm/error.rb`, `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb`
- HTTP connection: `lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb`
- Chat API: `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb`
- Test matrices: `spec/support/models_to_test.rb`

## Step 2: Trace a request to its concrete service integration

1. `RubyLLM.chat(model: ...)` — `lib/ruby_llm.rb:58`
2. `Chat.new(...)` — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:12`
3. `with_model` resolves model + provider — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:84`
4. `Models.resolve(...)` — `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:132`
5. Provider class lookup — `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:134`
6. `Provider.new(config)` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:13`
7. `Chat#complete` — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:179`
8. `provider_completion` — `lib/ruby_llm/chat.rb:295`
9. `Provider#complete` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:54`
10. `resolve_protocol` picks protocol — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:210`
11. Protocol instantiated and called — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:57`
12. `Protocol#complete` — `lib/ruby_llm/protocol.rb:20`

## Step 3: Map the shared API-dialect layer and its customizations

The shared dialect is `RubyLLM::Protocols::ChatCompletions` (`lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions.rb`), composed of modules in `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/`:

- `chat.rb` — payload rendering, response parsing, message/role/thinking formatting
- `models.rb` — `models` endpoint parsing
- `embeddings.rb` — embeddings payload/response
- `moderation.rb` — moderation endpoint
- `images.rb` — image generation/edits
- `streaming.rb` — SSE chunk parsing
- `tools.rb` — tool formatting/choice/argument parsing
- `media.rb` — image/audio/document/text attachment formatting
- `transcription.rb` — audio transcription
- `temperature.rb` — model-specific temperature normalization

**Existing chat_completions providers and their customizations:**

- **OpenAI** (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/openai.rb:15`) — switches audio/realtime/search-preview models to `chat_completions`; `openai_use_system_role` config changes `format_role` in `lib/ruby_llm/protocols/chat_completions/chat.rb:216`
- **XAI** (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/xai/chat.rb:9`) — bypasses system/developer role split; disables documents/audio in media (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/xai/chat.rb:14`); custom model parsing (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/xai/models.rb:10`)
- **DeepSeek** (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/deepseek/chat.rb:10`) — bypasses system/developer role; disables documents/images/audio (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/deepseek/chat.rb:14`)
- **Mistral** (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral/chat.rb`) — bypasses system/developer role; custom message formatting with thinking blocks; removes `stream_options`; maps `:required` tool choice to `'any'`; custom thinking payload logic; custom media (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/mistral/media.rb`); custom embeddings dropping `dimensions`; custom models parsing using capabilities
- **Ollama** (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/ollama/media.rb`) — only images + text attachments with `detail: auto`; hardcoded model capabilities in `lib/ruby_llm/providers/ollama/models.rb:12`
- **OpenRouter** (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter/`) — custom payload removing `reasoning_effort` and schema `strict`; custom reasoning object; custom thinking extraction; custom streaming chunk; image generation via chat endpoint; custom models parsing from architecture/pricing
- **Perplexity** (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity/`) — bypasses system/developer role; custom `file_url` document formatting; hardcoded model list
- **Azure** (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure/chat_completions.rb`) — endpoint routing based on `api_base` shape; embeddings wraps `input` in array; media supports images/audio/text only
- **GPUStack** (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/gpustack.rb:8`) — subclasses `Ollama::ChatCompletions` with its own `Models` module

## Step 4: Find how an integration declares and validates its settings

Each provider declares options via class methods:

- `configuration_options` — e.g. `lib/ruby_llm/providers/openai.rb:32`
- `configuration_requirements` — e.g. `lib/ruby_llm/providers/openai.rb:42`
- Optional overrides: `configured?` (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure.rb:30`), `ensure_configured!` (`lib/ruby_llm/providers/azure.rb:40`)

Registration path:

- `Provider.register` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:174`
- Registers options on `Configuration` via `register_provider_options` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:176` and `lib/ruby_llm/configuration.rb:25`

Validation path:

- `Provider#initialize` calls `ensure_configured!` — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:15`
- `ensure_configured!` checks `configuration_requirements` against config — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:237`
- `Provider.configured?` is the predicate — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:159`

## Step 5: Trace how HTTP failures become typed errors

- `Connection` registers the `:llm_errors` middleware — `lib/ruby_llm/connection.rb:117`
- Middleware defined in `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb`
- `ErrorMiddleware#call` invokes `parse_error` on completion — `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:14`
- `parse_error` first calls `provider.parse_error(response)` to extract a message — `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:41`
- Base provider error extraction — `lib/ruby_llm/provider.rb:104`
- Provider overrides: Bedrock `lib/ruby_llm/providers/bedrock.rb:24`, OpenRouter `lib/ruby_llm/providers/openrouter.rb:27`, Perplexity `lib/ruby_llm/providers/perplexity.rb:26`

Status-code mapping — `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:44`:

- `400` → `ContextLengthExceededError` or `BadRequestError`
- `401` → `UnauthorizedError`
- `402` → `PaymentRequiredError`
- `403` → `ForbiddenError`
- `429` → `RateLimitError` / `ContextLengthExceededError`
- `500` → `ServerError`
- `502–504` → `ServiceUnavailableError`
- `529` → `OverloadedError`
- else → `RubyLLM::Error`

Error classes defined in `lib/ruby_llm/error.rb:42`. Context/rate-limit patterns in `lib/ruby_llm/error_middleware.rb:21`. Streaming errors are reconstructed and fed to the same middleware from `lib/ruby_llm/streaming.rb:125`.

## Step 6: Find every site that must change to add a new integration

- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18` — add Zeitwerk inflection if needed
- `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116` — add `Provider.register :newprovider, RubyLLM::Providers::NewProvider`
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider.rb` — new provider class
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/*.rb` — optional customization modules
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:11-22` — add entry to `MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP` if models.dev supports it
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:26-40` — add to `PROVIDER_PREFERENCE`
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — add model entries or refresh registry
- `spec/support/models_to_test.rb` — add to test matrices (`CHAT_MODELS`, `VISION_MODELS`, etc.)
- `spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb:6-83` — add `api_base_cases` entry
- `spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb:86-120` — add `config_for` case
- `spec/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/` — add unit specs
- `.env.example` — document env vars

## Step 7: Produce the edit map

### Create

- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider.rb` — provider class; declare `protocol :chat_completions, NewProvider::ChatCompletions`; `api_base`; `headers`; `configuration_options`; `configuration_requirements`; optional `capabilities` and `parse_error`
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/chat.rb` — only if overriding `format_role`, `format_content`, `render_payload`, `parse_completion_response`, thinking/tool handling
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/models.rb` — only if overriding model-list parsing
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/media.rb` — only if attachment support differs from shared default
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/embeddings.rb` — only if embeddings payload/response differ
- `lib/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/capabilities.rb` — only if registry fallback capabilities are needed
- `spec/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/models_spec.rb`
- `spec/ruby_llm/providers/newprovider/chat_spec.rb` (if custom chat behavior)
- VCR cassettes generated by running integration specs

### Modify

- `lib/ruby_llm.rb`
  - `lib/ruby_llm.rb:18` — add inflection
  - `lib/ruby_llm.rb:104-116` — add `Provider.register` line
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb`
  - `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:11-22` — extend `MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP`
  - `lib/ruby_llm/models.rb:26-40` — extend `PROVIDER_PREFERENCE`
- `lib/ruby_llm/models.json` — append model entries or run `RubyLLM.models.refresh!` and `save_to_json`
- `spec/support/models_to_test.rb` — add representative model to relevant matrices
- `spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb`
  - `spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb:6-83` — add provider to `api_base_cases`
  - `spec/ruby_llm/provider_spec.rb:86-120` — add branch to `config_for`
- `.env.example` — add credential env vars

---
